41e6e21f-7458-4eb1-ac77-eb68df31d787.text: You experienced the impact of #TLM25 in person or online, and now it's time to see the benefits as an AASLD member. From three journal publications, Special Interest Groups (SIGs), and online learning with Liver Learning ®, to discounted rates for our exclusive Emerging Topic Conferences (ETCs), your membership connects you to the resources and community that advance your career year-round.

a589aeff-37b0-4844-a5b4-c3239c403101.text: [Join Today](https://www.aasld.org/become-member) Resmetirom and Semaglutide for MASH in 2025: Updated Recommendations from AASLD Guidance Stay up to date on the latest advances in MASLD/MASH management with this webinar that highlights new data on resmetirom—including real-world coverage, adherence, and safety—as well as emerging evidence on long-term use in both non-cirrhotic and cirrhotic MASH.

Apply for one of the six available awards to fund your innovative research. Additional dedicated funding is available for research in autoimmune hepatitis. Applications are due Monday, Dec. 8.

b37b79af-0908-4672-b327-758e4f4893ec.text: ](https://wkf.ms/3LYG0f4) Attend The Liver Meeting! No Matter Your Role in Hepatology—This Is Where You Belong Whether you're a clinician, surgeon, researcher, trainee, advocate, academic, or policymaker — The Liver Meeting in Washington, D.C., Nov. 7 - 11, is where the global hepatology community comes together. With access to the latest breakthroughs in liver disease research, practical tools for patient care, and a powerful network of experts shaping the future of hepatology, the program offers something for everyone committed to improving liver health.

[Home](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/) Become a Member Become a Member Joining AASLD means becoming a part of the premier organization committed to preventing and curing liver disease.

f5111792-35b9-455a-8838-152f5c89f5e6.text: Our members are committed hepatologists, researchers, surgeons, scientists, trainees and other health care professionals, who are dedicated to advancing the science of liver disease, making AASLD the leader in education, advocacy, and career development.

6d6a99fe-0217-4c52-9e95-acf9435d236c.text: What You’ll Need to Become a Member Gather your files—a current CV or, if you are applying as a Trainee, a letter from your Program Director Create an account Once you’re logged in, click “membership application” to start the process Upload your CV or Trainee information Ready to Become a Member? [Join AASLD](https://my.aasld.org/join) After you have submitted an application, please allow for up to two weeks for your membership to be finalized.

9f65b39f-5616-4d1a-b320-37400bd54875.text: With AASLD... Connect Events: The Liver Meeting® (TLM), Digestive Disease Week® (DDW), and Emerging Topic Conferences (ETC) Advocacy and public policy collaboration Over a dozen global sister societies Career Center job postings and resume bank Grow Free or discounted access to AASLD’s three premier scientific journals Access to Practice Guidelines by disease Liver Learning on-demand, expert-led content Research and career development awards through AASLD Foundation Lead Become a journal author Submit an abstract Lead a webinar Join a SIG or Committee Become an AASLD Fellow Recognize peers with AASLD Distinguished Awards [View All Benefits](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/membership-benefits) Renew Your AASLD Membership AASLD appreciates the continued support of our members.

6b42627d-7681-4aed-88a2-f04116dd71c3.text: Renew your AASLD membership today to stay informed on the latest research and developments in hepatology, network with thought leaders and colleagues in the field, advance your career, and save money through exclusive member discounts on AASLD meetings and events. Log in to your AASLD account to verify your membership status, renew your membership, and/or update your member profile with current information. Or, if you have your dues invoice and membership ID number, find your invoice and pay online.

fb7f25a7-b2ce-496c-b8c7-85c4020b9f08.text: Log in to your AASLD account to verify your membership status, renew your membership, and/or update your member profile with current information. Or, if you have your dues invoice and membership ID number, find your invoice and pay online. Still have questions or need assistance? We’re here to help! Contact AASLD’s Customer Service Center at 1-703-299-9766 or [aasld@aasld.org](mailto:aasld@aasld.org) . Team members are available to assist you Monday through Friday, from 9:00 am to 5:00 pm (Eastern). [Renew Now!

An Emeritus Member Must : Be 65 years old; Have retired from full-time employment; and Have a minimum of 10 consecutive years of membership in good standing immediately preceding the request Members interested in Emeritus membership can download this [request form](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/sites/default/files/2022-09/2019-AASLD-EmeritusMembershipApplication.pdf) and return via email to [membership@aasld.org](mailto:membership@aasld.org) .

3da718b4-ffb5-4eae-b917-a14e589a5789.text: Steering Committee positions can be occupied by AASLD membership categories that are in good standing and are SIG members. Chair The Chair plays a crucial leadership role within the SIG, overseeing the steering committee and ensuring effective governance and decision-making.

c107e524-6d8c-4e44-a848-42eeac4bef94.text: Key responsibilities include: Leadership: The Chair is the primary leader of the SIG steering committee, guiding the committee’s function to achieve the goals of the SIG membership and ensuring that it remains aligned with the strategic goals of the SIG and the organization.

290b8888-cf96-4230-993b-470309e91bda.text: Collaboration with the Governing Board: The Chair may be requested to support additional tasks from the AASLD Governing Board and will ensure that the committee’s work is aligned with the broader organizational goals, and periodic updates are reported back to the Board. Quarterly Calls: The Chair organizes and leads quarterly steering committee calls, collaborating with a staff representative to set the agenda and ensuring productive discussions.

a3c5b09e-07c5-4c05-9905-62697224a54b.text: The Chair’s role is critical in ensuring the SIG’s success by p roviding leadership, fostering collaboration, and aligning the steering committee’s work with the organization’s overarching goals. Following their term, chairs will be invited to serve as a member of the SIG Advisory Working Group. Vice-Chair The Vice-Chair plays a vital support role within the SIG, working closely with and assisting the Chair in various leadership and administrative functions.

dfb48351-4ecc-4345-ade2-a8db227694bd.text: Following their term, chairs will be invited to serve as a member of the SIG Advisory Working Group. Key responsibilities include: Support to the Chair: Working closely with the Chair to help oversee the steering committee’s activities. This includes assisting with planning, decision-making, and ensuring the smooth operation of SIG functions.

7b7dea54-58d7-4276-9759-f59a92406aa0.text: They are structured to provide interaction among members in a wide range of areas. Committee members deal with the issues and challenges affecting their area of expertise within the field, and help devise strategies, plans, and programs as directed by the Governing Board and the AASLD strategic plan. Each committee is comprised of current AASLD members and a Governing Board liaison as well as an AASLD staff member who serves as the liaison to the committee in the role of a staff advisor.

[Home](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/) Photo and Video Disclaimer Photo and Video Disclaimer AASLD plans to take photographs, audio, and video during our in-person events, and reserves the right to use any photograph/video taken at any AASLD event without the expressed written permission of those included within the photograph/video.

0f6e9d2c-6bbd-4d52-8900-34caf9d90414.text: AASLD may use the photograph/video in AASLD educational, news, or promotional materials, whether in print, electronic, or other forms of media, including on our website. To ensure the privacy of individuals, images will not be identified using full names or personal identifying information without written approval from the photographed subject.

5a2f2ee9-fda7-4509-9518-f98ce29e3d95.text: A person attending an AASLD event (or one we are participating in) who does not wish to have their image recorded for distribution should make their wishes known to the photographer, and/or the event organizers, and/or contact AASLD at [media@aasld.org](mailto:media@aasld.org) and include an identifying photograph for reference. AASLD will use the photo for identification purposes (only) and will hold the request in confidence.

0168ddf5-8abc-48c2-9fbc-cbe8bf321228.text: By participating in an AASLD event or by failing to notify AASLD, in writing, your desire to not have your photograph used by AASLD, you are agreeing to release, defend, hold harmless and indemnify AASLD from any and all claims involving the use of your picture or likeness. Have Questions?

Memoriam](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/memoriam) [Home](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/) Hepatology Training Pathways Hepatology Training Pathways By achieving American Board of Internal Medicine (ABIM) and/or American Board of Pediatric (ABP) certification in transplant hepatology, you demonstrate your commitment to the profession and show the public, your patients, your institution, and your colleagues that you have attained expertise in advanced and transplant hepatology beyond the general hepatology competence expected of a gastroenterologist.

d18e96b1-96ba-4ef5-9042-2c3803f4aaec.text: Certification in transplant hepatology is the preferred pathway for the expert hepatologist, whether your career goals involve practice in an academic liver transplant program or a community general hepatology practice.

600197cb-e448-4740-a3a4-9541778f7e4f.text: Advertisement Transplant Hepatology Fellowship Programs There are two pathways available to achieve certification in transplant hepatology: Transplant hepatology training after gastroenterology fellowship (“fourth year”) Dual certification training in transplant hepatology during gastroenterology fellowship (formerly known as the “Pilot”) There are over 60 ACGME-accredited fellowships in transplant hepatology currently available.

3650e393-26e0-43d7-94ab-8c5e9c1d9f28.text: Most but not all transplant hepatology fellowship programs currently offer the dual certification pathway.

58bedbc1-e175-4a6b-a409-c187233b672b.text: Transplant Hepatology Training After Gastroenterology Fellowship ("Fourth Year") One year of advanced training for those who have completed a three-year fellowship in gastroenterology (total of four years for completion of gastroenterology and transplant hepatology fellowship training) Benefits of an additional year of fellowship training after completion of a gastroenterology fellowship include: Advanced training in general and transplant hepatology More time available for training and development of competence in gastroenterology More time to pursue scholarly activities including research and/or an advanced degree during the three-year gastroenterology fellowship Opportunity to pursue advanced training at a different institution Ideal for trainees interested in an academic hepatology career, advanced degree, and/or a career with a significant research focus When and How to Apply Visit the [AASLD Transplant Hepatology Fellowship Application System](https://www.aasldapp.org/) to search for available transplant hepatology fellowship positions.

88760506-974a-4acf-bd88-472292e6bbd2.text: You can reach out to learn more about programs and/or submit applications to participating programs through the site Programs can be viewed and applications submitted in January-March of the year before anticipated start of transplant hepatology fellowship training Interviews take place in March and April Match Day is in May of the year prior to start of training Visit the [site](https://www.aasldapp.org/) for the current schedule and to search for participating programs Dual Certification Training in Transplant Hepatology During Gastroenterology Fellowship (Formerly Known As The “Pilot”) Transplant hepatology training during the three-year gastroenterology fellowship (total of three years for completion of gastroenterology and transplant hepatology fellowship training) Benefits of dual-certification training include: Advanced training in general and transplant hepatology Less time in training that may not align with career interests Shortened overall duration of training Ideal for trainees interested in a clinically-focused hepatology career When and How to Apply The dual certification pathway is available to existing gastroenterology fellows at a program with an accredited transplant hepatology fellowship program at the same institution In some programs, this pathway may be competitive depending on the number of interested fellows and other program-specific factors Discuss your interest in this pathway with gastroenterology fellowship program directors during interviews and/or during your first year of gastroenterology fellowship See [ABIM](https://www.abim.org/certification/policies/internal-medicine-subspecialty-policies/transplant-hepatology.aspx) and [ACGME](https://www.acgme.org/specialties/internal-medicine/program-requirements-and-faqs-and-applications/) websites for program requirements Transplant Hepatology Certification Upon completing transplant hepatology training and passing the gastroenterology board certification, fellows will be eligible to take the [Transplant Hepatology Board Certification Exam](https://www.abim.org/certification/exam-information/transplant-hepatology) jointly offered by the ABIM and ABP The dual certification pathway is currently only eligible for certification through the ABIM and is not available through the ABP Have Questions?

da2c4a93-4c03-4959-9647-fa9d89344151.text: About the Project Portal The [AASLD Project Portal](https://aasldprograms.secure-platform.com/site) is a brand-new platform for members to easily submit proposals for white papers, projects, and initiatives that align with AASLD’s mission and [strategic priorities](https://www.aasld.org/sites/default/files/2025-02/image.jpeg) . This portal is designed to foster innovation and streamline how projects are submitted, reviewed, and brought to life to advance liver health.

fb80debb-640f-4c5b-ae2f-8c18c5f5124b.text: About the Project Innovation Awards The inaugural Project Innovation Awards will fund high-impact ideas that demonstrate promise in advancing liver health, education, or community engagement, submitted through the Project Portal. Funding for the Project Innovation Awards is provided by the [AASLD Foundation](https://www.aasldfoundation.org/) thanks to the generous donation by an anonymous donor. Submissions are now open through Sept. 10 in the [Project Portal!

The Project Innovation Awards are donor-funded grants awarded to high-impact member-led proposals. Awarded projects receive funding and recognition for their innovation and potential to benefit the liver health community. The awards are designed to promote new or innovative programs; these are not research awards.

2237dc34-d7a9-44cb-a53d-d9e5aa988ed9.text: Who can submit? All active AASLD members are eligible to submit proposals through the portal. Do you need to renew your membership to access this exclusive member benefit? Click [here](https://my.aasld.org/renew) ! When are submissions due? Submissions are due by Sept. 10. How will project submissions be evaluated? First, AASLD staff will conduct an initial eligibility review to include such criteria as current membership. From there, committees or SIGs will be asked to provide input and endorsements for proposals.
2237dc34-d7a9-44cb-a53d-d9e5aa988ed9.fileName: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD

03f1c45a-2458-457d-839b-eb95dd62c62f.text: First, AASLD staff will conduct an initial eligibility review to include such criteria as current membership. From there, committees or SIGs will be asked to provide input and endorsements for proposals. Approved proposals will be evaluated by a selection committee comprised of AASLD Board and Foundation Board members. Submissions will be reviewed based on: Strategic Alignment : Demonstrates fit with AASLD’s strategic pillars. Mission Resonance : Showcases relevance to AASLD’s core goals and values.
03f1c45a-2458-457d-839b-eb95dd62c62f.fileName: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD

a377f7d0-9cd4-4a18-a511-4c79f88f3cd0.text: Submissions will be reviewed based on: Strategic Alignment : Demonstrates fit with AASLD’s strategic pillars. Mission Resonance : Showcases relevance to AASLD’s core goals and values. Resource Assessment : Details feasibility, cost, and required support. Committee/SIG Endorsement : Includes necessary support from relevant AASLD stakeholders. How do I get a project endorsement? Once you submit your proposal, you will receive a confirmation notice.
a377f7d0-9cd4-4a18-a511-4c79f88f3cd0.fileName: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD
a377f7d0-9cd4-4a18-a511-4c79f88f3cd0.topic: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD

3d9fc461-616c-4fb5-bb74-817689d61e2c.text: How do I get a project endorsement? Once you submit your proposal, you will receive a confirmation notice. Your proposal will be sent to the appropriate committee or SIG for review and endorsement. Upon completion of this review phase, the Chair of the relevant Committee or SIG will complete the endorsement form which will be part of the overall proposal package. This package will then be shared with the selection committee for evaluation.
3d9fc461-616c-4fb5-bb74-817689d61e2c.fileName: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD
3d9fc461-616c-4fb5-bb74-817689d61e2c.topic: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD

13d28282-57fe-41de-89b9-ff0e91f19892.text: Upon completion of this review phase, the Chair of the relevant Committee or SIG will complete the endorsement form which will be part of the overall proposal package. This package will then be shared with the selection committee for evaluation. Endorsements will be based on merit, feasibility, and strategic alignment. Members may be asked to present or answer questions to support their proposals. For more information about project endorsements, contact your AASLD Committee or SIG Staff Liaison.
13d28282-57fe-41de-89b9-ff0e91f19892.fileName: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD
13d28282-57fe-41de-89b9-ff0e91f19892.topic: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD

b74a3840-d204-4277-85e9-398fa6b45621.text: Members may be asked to present or answer questions to support their proposals. For more information about project endorsements, contact your AASLD Committee or SIG Staff Liaison. For more information about our committees and SIGs, please email [governance@aasld.org](mailto:governance@aasld.org) . What can Project Innovation Award grant funds be used for? Funds from the Project Innovation Awards can support: Project-related expenses, such as technology, software, printing, or hosting.
b74a3840-d204-4277-85e9-398fa6b45621.fileName: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD
b74a3840-d204-4277-85e9-398fa6b45621.topic: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD

f04f2957-8cb2-4355-8839-ee85e6676f25.text: What can Project Innovation Award grant funds be used for? Funds from the Project Innovation Awards can support: Project-related expenses, such as technology, software, printing, or hosting. External consultants or vendors (if needed for project delivery). Travel or honoraria for speakers or collaborators. Materials for education, outreach, or dissemination. What level of funding support can I request through the Project Innovation Awards?
f04f2957-8cb2-4355-8839-ee85e6676f25.fileName: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD
f04f2957-8cb2-4355-8839-ee85e6676f25.topic: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD

f538a1d2-06a4-4ee7-ae46-6577a6f15491.text: Materials for education, outreach, or dissemination. What level of funding support can I request through the Project Innovation Awards? Members may request any amount up to $36,000 in award funding to support eligible project costs. Funding requests should be proportionate to the project’s scope, impact, and alignment with AASLD’s strategic priorities. When requesting funding, be sure to include: A clear budget with projected expenses. A justification for how the funds will be used.
f538a1d2-06a4-4ee7-ae46-6577a6f15491.fileName: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD
f538a1d2-06a4-4ee7-ae46-6577a6f15491.topic: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD

28c27e64-93b3-47bb-9564-d4f98055369d.text: When requesting funding, be sure to include: A clear budget with projected expenses. A justification for how the funds will be used. An explanation of how the funding will advance the project's goals. Note: Proposals may be partially or fully funded depending on volume and donor selection. What is the estimated timeline for the Project Innovation Awards? July–September 2025: Submission window open. September 10, 2025: Submissions close. October 2025: Finalists selected by Governing Board; Donor selects awardees.
28c27e64-93b3-47bb-9564-d4f98055369d.fileName: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD
28c27e64-93b3-47bb-9564-d4f98055369d.topic: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD

30c4baf3-5a2a-47d7-92e9-4e5bc759b5ed.text: September 10, 2025: Submissions close. October 2025: Finalists selected by Governing Board; Donor selects awardees. November 2025: Awardees notified and recognized at The Liver Meeting . *Note: Timeline may shift depending on submission volume and donor approval. [White Paper Submissions](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/introducing-aasld-project-portal-project-innovation-awards#card-accordion-990028819) What should I consider before proposing a white paper?
30c4baf3-5a2a-47d7-92e9-4e5bc759b5ed.fileName: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD
30c4baf3-5a2a-47d7-92e9-4e5bc759b5ed.topic: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD

9dc38528-96a9-4173-a30c-26e1812e20f9.text: *Note: Timeline may shift depending on submission volume and donor approval. [White Paper Submissions](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/introducing-aasld-project-portal-project-innovation-awards#card-accordion-990028819) What should I consider before proposing a white paper? Before submitting a proposal, start by developing a clear concept. Define the purpose, target audience, and the relevance of your idea to AASLD’s mission and the field of hepatology. Ask yourself: What issue does the white paper address?
9dc38528-96a9-4173-a30c-26e1812e20f9.fileName: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD
9dc38528-96a9-4173-a30c-26e1812e20f9.topic: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD

13edc796-96ac-4943-bccb-c00ba251300e.text: Define the purpose, target audience, and the relevance of your idea to AASLD’s mission and the field of hepatology. Ask yourself: What issue does the white paper address? How will it contribute to the field or AASLD's strategic priorities? Who is the intended audience, and how will they benefit? You will also want to identify key themes and topics, consult with subject matter experts (if needed), and determine the paper’s length, structure, and scope. What should my white paper proposal include?
13edc796-96ac-4943-bccb-c00ba251300e.fileName: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD
13edc796-96ac-4943-bccb-c00ba251300e.topic: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD

b899478c-d86f-427e-aaf2-577c8e3c4867.text: You will also want to identify key themes and topics, consult with subject matter experts (if needed), and determine the paper’s length, structure, and scope. What should my white paper proposal include? Your proposal (no more than 750 words) should include: An executive summary with purpose, audience, relevance to AASLD’s strategic priorities, and anticipated impact. A detailed outline of the proposed white paper. A timeline for writing, review, and publication, with milestones and deadlines.
b899478c-d86f-427e-aaf2-577c8e3c4867.fileName: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD
b899478c-d86f-427e-aaf2-577c8e3c4867.topic: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD

f022a60c-719c-4d1a-a073-a49d0f9598fd.text: A detailed outline of the proposed white paper. A timeline for writing, review, and publication, with milestones and deadlines. A list of proposed authors and contributors. The intended publication outlet (e.g., AASLD journal, website, newsletter, etc.). Who should I consult before submitting my proposal? Before submitting through the portal, you should consult the Governing Board Liaison (GBL) assigned to your Committee or SIG. They will provide feedback and initial approval.
f022a60c-719c-4d1a-a073-a49d0f9598fd.fileName: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD
f022a60c-719c-4d1a-a073-a49d0f9598fd.topic: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD

d5b47f05-ed40-4907-9241-718c749b8775.text: Before submitting through the portal, you should consult the Governing Board Liaison (GBL) assigned to your Committee or SIG. They will provide feedback and initial approval. If your proposal comes from a SIG, it is strongly recommended to also present your draft to the SIG Advisory Committee for input. How do I get formal endorsement for my white paper?
d5b47f05-ed40-4907-9241-718c749b8775.fileName: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD
d5b47f05-ed40-4907-9241-718c749b8775.topic: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD

bd965b66-ed25-4ccd-9738-f6c019c317c5.text: If your proposal comes from a SIG, it is strongly recommended to also present your draft to the SIG Advisory Committee for input. How do I get formal endorsement for my white paper? Once your Governing Board Liaison has reviewed and supported the proposal: Submit it through the [AASLD Project Portal](https://aasldprograms.secure-platform.com/site) . AASLD staff will route your submission to the appropriate Committee or SIG. The Committee or SIG will formally discuss and vote on the proposal.
bd965b66-ed25-4ccd-9738-f6c019c317c5.fileName: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD
bd965b66-ed25-4ccd-9738-f6c019c317c5.topic: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD

df648c24-456b-4240-b190-6e52ccc08f8e.text: AASLD staff will route your submission to the appropriate Committee or SIG. The Committee or SIG will formally discuss and vote on the proposal. If approved (majority vote), the endorsement must be documented in meeting minutes or a written approval letter. The Committee/SIG Chair will upload or submit the endorsement in the online portal. What happens after my proposal is endorsed? Once endorsed, AASLD staff will share your proposal with the intended journal editor(s) .
df648c24-456b-4240-b190-6e52ccc08f8e.fileName: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD
df648c24-456b-4240-b190-6e52ccc08f8e.topic: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD

c12b94fd-942c-4765-a7f6-91df215249c0.text: What happens after my proposal is endorsed? Once endorsed, AASLD staff will share your proposal with the intended journal editor(s) . Editors will review and respond within approximately 2 weeks with: Acceptance/non-acceptance. A suggestion to submit to another AASLD journal or platform. Requested revisions or feedback. What comes next if the journal approves the white paper?
c12b94fd-942c-4765-a7f6-91df215249c0.fileName: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD
c12b94fd-942c-4765-a7f6-91df215249c0.topic: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD

50246d56-4276-47bc-98fa-232d0bd3d36b.text: Requested revisions or feedback. What comes next if the journal approves the white paper? Once the journal editor(s) approve your proposal, it moves into the next phases of development, including: Full manuscript drafting and internal/external review. Final review and approval by the AASLD Governing Board. Formal submission and approval by the journal editorial board. What if the journal does not accept the proposal?
50246d56-4276-47bc-98fa-232d0bd3d36b.fileName: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD
50246d56-4276-47bc-98fa-232d0bd3d36b.topic: Introducing the AASLD Project Portal & Project Innovation Awards | AASLD

607ca29b-31d0-4792-9121-0e423356052e.text: Formal submission and approval by the journal editorial board. What if the journal does not accept the proposal? If a journal declines to publish the white paper, you may consider adapting it as another AASLD resource—such as: LiverLearning® module Feature on the AASLD website Newsletter or member communication deliverable For questions or more information, please reach out to Michael Providence, Director, Policy & Planning, at [mprovidence@aasld.org](mailto:mprovidence@aasld.org) . Have Questions?

providers, and their patients.

c5609109-81ac-479f-af23-b05ec3bb505c.text: The following resources are designed to provide clinicians with clinical guidance and resources to share with patients.

4b304cd0-7047-4142-93dd-941400bd1251.text: [Resources for Patients](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/patient-resources) [On This Page :](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/covid-19-and-liver#anchor-nav-1384203184) [COVID-19 and the Liver Webinar Series](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/covid-19-and-liver#covid-19-and-the-liver-webinar-series) [Statement on COVID-19 Vaccines and Clinical Best Practice for Hepatology and Liver Transplant Providers](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/covid-19-and-liver#statement-on-covid-19-vaccines-and-clinical-best-practice-for-hepatology-and-liver-transplant-providers) [Joint Society Messages](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/covid-19-and-liver#joint-society-messages) [Additional Resources for Clinicians](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/covid-19-and-liver#additional-resources-for-clinicians) [Resources for Trainees and Early Career Health Care Professionals](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/covid-19-and-liver#resources-for-trainees-and-early-career-health-care-professionals) [What You Can Do](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/covid-19-and-liver#what-you-can-do) COVID-19 and the Liver Webinar Series In response to the COVID-19 pandemic, AASLD has created a series of webinars dedicated to ongoing discoveries and discussions about how it affects the care of liver patients.

9c660fcd-3117-40a8-ae6d-89bafa01b1b7.text: These webinars, including those in which we collaborated with sister organizations, are located on the "COVID-19 and the Liver Webinar Series" page.

4cbb35cc-fe16-46ac-a2f1-ad22ad4ff6c6.text: [View COVID-19 Webinar Library](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/covid19-webinars) Statement on COVID-19 Vaccines and Clinical Best Practice for Hepatology and Liver Transplant Providers This document provides guidance for hepatology and liver transplant providers for managing patients with liver disease and liver transplant recipients in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic.

6d4bb5ed-6c30-413f-ba43-c1858c5709ca.text: Our aim is to provide a template for developing clinical recommendations and policies to mitigate the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on liver patients and health care providers. This 12-page document updates and replaces 2 larger documents, "Clinical best practice advice for hepatology and liver transplant providers during the COVID-19 pandemic: AASLD expert panel consensus statement" and "AASLD expert panel consensus statement: Vaccines to prevent COVID-19 in patients with liver disease" .

547d17aa-e1c6-4909-9649-94d6f25be957.text: Download the expert panel consensus statement: [AASLD Expert Panel Consensus Statement: COVID-19 Clinical Best Practice Advice for Hepatology and Liver Transplant Providers [October 6, 2022]](https://www.aasld.org/sites/default/files/2022-10/AASLD%20COVID-19%20Guidance%20Document%2010.06.2022F.pdf) Joint Society Messages [Joint Society Message: Supplemental Funding for VA Research to Support COVID-19 Research Efforts Needed](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/sites/default/files/2022-04/FOVA-COVIDSupplementLetter-5.21.20-Final.pdf) [Joint Society Message: Use of Personal Protective Equipment in GI Endoscopy](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/sites/default/files/2022-04/JointSocietyMessage-PersonalProtectiveEquipmentInGIEndoscopy.pdf) [Joint Society Message: Gastroenterology Professional Society Guidance on Endoscopic Procedures during the COVID-19 Pandemic](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/sites/default/files/2022-04/JointSocietyMessage-ProceduresInTimeOfCOVID19-FINAL.pdf) [Joint GI Society Message: COVID-19 Clinical Insights for Our Community of Gastroenterologists and Gastroenterology Care Providers](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/news/joint-gi-society-message-covid-19-clinical-insights-our-community-gastroenterologists-and) Additional Resources for Clinicians [AASLD Journal Articles on COVID-19](https://aasldpubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/action/doSearch?AllField=COVID-19) [Mental Health and Burnout Resources](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/sites/default/files/2022-06/COVID%2BLiver%20-%20Mental%20Health%20Resource%20Guide_FINAL.pdf) Resources for Trainees and Early Career Health Care Professionals AASLD hosted a "Trainee and Early Career Experience in Hepatology during COVID-19" webinar.

UNC-Chapel Hill aims to assess outcomes of COVID-19 in patients with chronic liver disease and prior liver transplant including updated questions on the role of living conditions and employment, alcohol use, thromboembolic complications, novel COVID-19 therapies, emerging SARS-CoV-2 variants and post-vaccination infections.

95373fed-f6ae-464d-b9d7-db1fc71d655c.text: [See the Latest Posts](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/forums/home) Report Cases to the SECURE-Liver Registry . UNC-Chapel Hill aims to assess outcomes of COVID-19 in patients with chronic liver disease and prior liver transplant including updated questions on the role of living conditions and employment, alcohol use, thromboembolic complications, novel COVID-19 therapies, emerging SARS-CoV-2 variants and post-vaccination infections. To help further their research, report cases (hyperlinked text for “report cases”) of COVID-19 in chronic liver disease and post-liver transplant patients, regardless of severity of COVID-19 (including asymptomatic patients detected through public health screening).

To help further their research, report cases (hyperlinked text for “report cases”) of COVID-19 in chronic liver disease and post-liver transplant patients, regardless of severity of COVID-19 (including asymptomatic patients detected through public health screening). [Home](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/) Clinician Resources Clinician Resources AASLD offers a variety of resources for hepatologists and other clinicians.

19f17e71-a975-47a1-9624-04d93954103a.text: These resources are available to enhance knowledge and improve quality care in the hepatology field. Every resource is curated with the audience in mind and complimentary access is granted with an [AASLD membership](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/become-member) .

a86dc077-20d1-4f46-bd41-46581b677f5b.text: Providers Clinical Support System - Medications for Alcohol Use Disorder Providers Clinical Support System – Medications for Alcohol Use Disorder is a national project funded by the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration to provide free, comprehensive training, guidance, and mentoring on the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of alcohol use disorder.

4edaef1b-1cef-4584-a953-1f338c64b579.text: PCSS-MAUD consists of a coalition of leading national organizations that educate and advocate for healthcare professionals, while actively addressing the challenges posed by alcohol use disorder. As the experts in liver care, AASLD is a supporting partner for the PCSS-MAUD initiative. [Access Free Resource](https://www.pcss-maud.org/) Liver Learning [LiverLearning®](https://liverlearning.aasld.org/) is the premier source for hepatology educational content for clinicians and other healthcare professionals.

8e55aa12-2aaf-4773-8fef-4f1475766d33.text: As the experts in liver care, AASLD is a supporting partner for the PCSS-MAUD initiative. [Access Free Resource](https://www.pcss-maud.org/) Liver Learning [LiverLearning®](https://liverlearning.aasld.org/) is the premier source for hepatology educational content for clinicians and other healthcare professionals. Access educational resources and earn continuing education credits (CME/CE credits and MOC points) from online enduring activities. Current AASLD members automatically receive complimentary access.

179e2b9b-d88f-4f32-89eb-5947bdb92404.text: Access educational resources and earn continuing education credits (CME/CE credits and MOC points) from online enduring activities. [Read the HEPATOLOGY Article](https://aasldpubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/ftr/10.1002/hep.32110) Use of Skeletal Muscle Index as a Predictor of Wait-List Mortality in Patients With End-Stage Liver Disease The aim of this study is to validate a proposed definition of sarcopenia in predicting wait-list mortality.

fcb11748-4216-4fa4-b25f-177cce6729db.text: Hepatitis B Research Network The [Hepatitis B Research Network](https://repository.niddk.nih.gov/network/110) brings together clinical centers with expertise in caring for patients with chronic hepatitis B virus (HBV) infection. Hepatitis B Online Community [HepBcommunity.org](https://www.hepbcommunity.org/) is a global online forum dedicated to supporting those living with and affected by Hepatitis B. It offers a free platform to connect with each other, form support networks, and get advice from verified medical experts and researchers.

e1ff0104-06b0-4080-9133-cb5d23ced29b.text: Hepatitis B Online Community [HepBcommunity.org](https://www.hepbcommunity.org/) is a global online forum dedicated to supporting those living with and affected by Hepatitis B. It offers a free platform to connect with each other, form support networks, and get advice from verified medical experts and researchers. It is funded and coordinated by the Storr Liver Center, Westmead Hospital Foundation, and Hepatitis B Foundation with support from the World Hepatitis Alliance.

c86033a0-0593-4ee5-bb01-75d6c83e22ad.text: It offers a free platform to connect with each other, form support networks, and get advice from verified medical experts and researchers. It is funded and coordinated by the Storr Liver Center, Westmead Hospital Foundation, and Hepatitis B Foundation with support from the World Hepatitis Alliance. (Note: It is not intended to replace doctor and specialist consultations for the medical care or treatment of Hepatitis B) [Hepatitis C](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/patient-resources#card-accordion-1707554464) CDC Recommendation — Hepatitis C Testing for Anyone Born During 1945–1965 The CDC recommends that everyone born between 1945–1965 [also known as baby boomers] [get a blood test for Hepatitis C](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5777166/) .

dd0ee5b9-a053-46f5-8c7e-6bc250e3c143.text: It is funded and coordinated by the Storr Liver Center, Westmead Hospital Foundation, and Hepatitis B Foundation with support from the World Hepatitis Alliance. This recommendation calls for one-time testing of baby boomers.

1126ad1c-c040-4d82-8544-2c395b842f10.text: [Organ Donation](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/patient-resources#card-accordion-600529450) The liver is critical to a person's well-being. More than 25,000,000 Americans – one in every 10 – are afflicted with liver-related diseases each year. More than 27,000 Americans die each year from chronic liver diseases and cirrhosis. Lives depend on organ and tissue donations and there is a severe shortage. Thousands of children and adults are waiting for organs every day, and the numbers are increasing.

91193adc-1029-44f3-8935-0fc633854e36.text: Lives depend on organ and tissue donations and there is a severe shortage. Thousands of children and adults are waiting for organs every day, and the numbers are increasing. Liver donors are usually persons who have died and whose families have consented to have their organs donated. Today, a living person can donate a portion of his/her liver to another person. The liver is the only organ that can regenerate itself.

304a6c9d-6814-4701-a549-05949c0b942a.text: Today, a living person can donate a portion of his/her liver to another person. The liver is the only organ that can regenerate itself. Liver transplantation, once impossible, is now highly feasible, and there have been dramatic gains in the survival rates of recipients.

8be709a1-e98a-4717-9c66-d8b826a403bb.text: The liver is the only organ that can regenerate itself. Liver transplantation, once impossible, is now highly feasible, and there have been dramatic gains in the survival rates of recipients. For more information on liver donations, contact the following organizations: [United Network for Organ Sharing Transplant Statistics](https://unos.org/data/transplant-trends/) [American Liver Foundation](http://www.liverfoundation.org/) [Viral Hepatitis](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/patient-resources#card-accordion-267412799) Viral Hepatitis in the United States: The Road to Elimination Leadership from the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) and its Centers for Disease Control & Prevention (CDC) presented exciting updates from the Viral Hepatitis National Strategic Plan for the United States at The Liver Meeting Digital Experience™ (TLMdX) 2020.

e06f07af-7a59-4bb5-befa-f20927f52fd8.text: Liver transplantation, once impossible, is now highly feasible, and there have been dramatic gains in the survival rates of recipients. For more information on liver donations, contact the following organizations: [United Network for Organ Sharing Transplant Statistics](https://unos.org/data/transplant-trends/) [American Liver Foundation](http://www.liverfoundation.org/) [Viral Hepatitis](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/patient-resources#card-accordion-267412799) Viral Hepatitis in the United States: The Road to Elimination Leadership from the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) and its Centers for Disease Control & Prevention (CDC) presented exciting updates from the Viral Hepatitis National Strategic Plan for the United States at The Liver Meeting Digital Experience™ (TLMdX) 2020. They highlighted areas of impact and opportunities, discussed how it will be implemented, and facilitated a robust conversation with TLMdX attendees.

d9cfe727-edf7-4d91-a911-e28d626ec679.text: For more information on liver donations, contact the following organizations: [United Network for Organ Sharing Transplant Statistics](https://unos.org/data/transplant-trends/) [American Liver Foundation](http://www.liverfoundation.org/) [Viral Hepatitis](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/patient-resources#card-accordion-267412799) Viral Hepatitis in the United States: The Road to Elimination Leadership from the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) and its Centers for Disease Control & Prevention (CDC) presented exciting updates from the Viral Hepatitis National Strategic Plan for the United States at The Liver Meeting Digital Experience™ (TLMdX) 2020. They highlighted areas of impact and opportunities, discussed how it will be implemented, and facilitated a robust conversation with TLMdX attendees. The Viral Hepatitis National Strategic Plan for the United States lays out a Roadmap to Hepatitis Elimination for the nation.

cec8be0c-e3b1-4ad4-be76-ed607dbb3f79.text: They highlighted areas of impact and opportunities, discussed how it will be implemented, and facilitated a robust conversation with TLMdX attendees. The Viral Hepatitis National Strategic Plan for the United States lays out a Roadmap to Hepatitis Elimination for the nation. The plan, grounded in the latest science, guides stakeholders from all sectors in collaborative efforts focused on key strategies to achieve national viral hepatitis elimination goals.

61869151-cabe-47f3-9047-0fbed49caee7.text: The Viral Hepatitis National Strategic Plan for the United States lays out a Roadmap to Hepatitis Elimination for the nation. The plan, grounded in the latest science, guides stakeholders from all sectors in collaborative efforts focused on key strategies to achieve national viral hepatitis elimination goals. 6defbc42-6410-4555-9eb0-d06baab5d2b1.text: Experience the new era of publishing with AASLD! When selecting a home for your research, we understand the importance of ensuring that your paper aligns with the journal's scope, secures the highest impact and benefits from rigorous peer review standards to ensure quality. Why publish in an AASLD journal?

09fc2c9b-ad94-4e40-91e9-f135bdd15dab.text: When selecting a home for your research, we understand the importance of ensuring that your paper aligns with the journal's scope, secures the highest impact and benefits from rigorous peer review standards to ensure quality. Why publish in an AASLD journal? -Format-free first submissions -Instant feedback to improve your manuscript with Paperpal Preflight -Rapid review and publication -Graphics support for all accepted articles -Professionally designed graphical abstract templates -Easy, efficient transfers within the AASLD portfolio of journals with expedited review and decision -Promotion of articles through journals’ social media channels HEPATOLOGY The premier publication in the field of liver disease, HEPATOLOGY publishes original, peer-reviewed articles concerning all aspects of liver structure, function and disease.

ddd37d70-3ed8-4b00-99db-5143a03107df.text: The American Association for the Study of Liver Diseases (AASLD) announces the updated Practice Guidance on Semaglutide therapy for Metabolic November 6, 2025 [Statement from AASLD: AASLD Acknowledges Withdrawal of Obeticholic Acid for Primary Biliary Cholangitis and Announces Upcoming Guideline Update](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/statement-aasld-aasld-acknowledges-withdrawal-obeticholic-acid-primary-biliary-cholangitis-and) The American Association for the Study of Liver Diseases (AASLD) acknowledges the recent market withdrawal of obeticholic acid (OCA) for the treatment of primary biliary cholangitis (PBC) by Intercept Pharmaceuticals.

ab082168-d0ed-42e9-9f01-55ecf6451493.text: OCA has served as a second-line therapeutic option for patients with an inadequate response to ursodeoxycholic acid, and its withdrawal marks a significant change in the PBC November 4, 2025 [AASLD Announces New Practice Guideline on Treatment of Chronic Hepatitis B](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-announces-new-practice-guideline-treatment-chronic-hepatitis-b) The American Association for the Study of Liver Diseases (AASLD) and the Infectious Diseases Society of America (IDSA) are pleased to announce a new Practice Guideline on Treatment of Chronic Hepatitis B published in HEPATOLOGY, the premier publication in the field of liver disease.

01ba62e1-8add-47b8-b89d-9d1fece72e55.text: The new guideline provides October 7, 2025 [AASLD Announces the 2025 Distinguished Advocacy Award Recipient](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-announces-2025-distinguished-advocacy-award-recipient) AASLD proudly recognizes the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) Hepatitis Innovation Team (HIT) and the HIT collaborating offices with VA ALEXANDRIA, Virginia — Each year, the American Association for the Study of Liver Diseases (AASLD) presents its most prestigious awards to recognize exceptional leadership, mentorship, service, and advocacy across the liver community.

a58e91df-77f5-42e5-ae49-6de4bedc82f6.text: AASLD proudly announces September 18, 2025 [Advancing Science in Cholestatic Liver Diseases: A HEPATOLOGY Special Issue](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/advancing-science-cholestatic-liver-diseases-hepatology-special-issue) ALEXANDRIA, Virginia — The American Association for the Study of Liver Diseases (AASLD) is pleased to share the Special Issue on Cholestasis published by HEPATOLOGY, the premier publication in the field of liver disease.

ead42887-551c-4e41-97d9-78f4dd7b47eb.text: Cholestatic liver diseases—such as primary sclerosing cholangitis (PSC), primary biliary cholangitis (PBC), and IgG4-related cholangitis—pose a significant but often September 12, 2025 [AASLD Announces the 2025 Distinguished Award Recipients](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-announces-2025-distinguished-award-recipients) Alexandria, Virginia — The American Association for the Study of Liver Diseases (AASLD) proudly announces the 2025 Distinguished Award recipients, honoring individuals whose outstanding contributions have shaped the field of hepatology and advanced the care of patients with liver disease.

7b520ecc-aa8a-4bfa-b126-604369d1221d.text: Each year, AASLD presents its most prestigious awards to recognize exceptional leadership, mentorship, service August 22, 2025 [AASLD and AST Announce New Practice Guideline on Adult Liver Transplantation: Diagnosis and Management of Graft-Related Complications](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-and-ast-announce-new-practice-guideline-adult-liver-transplantation-diagnosis-and-management) ALEXANDRIA, Virginia — The American Association for the Study of Liver Diseases (AASLD) and the American Society of Transplantation (AST) are pleased to announce the latest Practice Guideline on Adult Liver Transplantation: Diagnosis and Management of Graft-Related Complications published in Liver Transplantation, the premier journal equipping clinicians and researchers with the latest insights in August 19, 2025 [AASLD Applauds FDA Approval of First GLP-1 Therapy for MASH, Expanding Use of Popular Weight Loss Drug to Liver Disease](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-applauds-fda-approval-first-glp-1-therapy-mash-expanding-use-popular-weight-loss-drug-liver) The American Association for the Study of Liver Diseases (AASLD) applauds the U.S. Food and Drug Administration’s (FDA) recent approval of Wegovy®, widely recognized for weight loss and diabetes management, as a new treatment for adults with Metabolic dysfunction-Associated Steatohepatitis (MASH) and moderate to advanced liver fibrosis.

c144fb77-159e-4264-aa5d-9976932a219b.text: This investment will fuel innovative research to advance the understanding, prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of June 17, 2025 [AASLD Announces 2026 Leadership](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-announces-2026-leadership) Incoming Councilor (2026-2031) Incoming Treasurer (2026-2028) For more information on the new leadership and to view the candidates for Councilor at Large, Chair Ethics Committee, and the 2026 Nominating Committee, please click here.

5f35596e-d089-44ff-ac24-3a5697d410ad.text: March 25, 2025 [AASLD Announces New Class of 2025 Fellows](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-announces-new-class-2025-fellows) AASLD is pleased to announce the selection of thirty-seven hepatology leaders as Fellows and four allied health leaders focused on hepatology as Associate Fellows.

ed3dd238-cb5f-47ff-ae84-047405242fd0.text: The Fellows Programs bestows honorary recognition upon AASLD Regular and International members who have made a significant contribution to the knowledge and/or practice of liver and biliary diseases, and are considered leaders in the March 18, 2025 [AASLD Opens Call for Applications for Sites to Participate in TARGET-LD and the Cirrhosis Quality Collaborative (CQC Data Registry)](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-opens-call-applications-sites-participate-target-ld-and-cirrhosis-quality-collaborative-cqc) Alexandria, VA – [March 18, 2025] – The American Association for the Study of Liver Diseases ( AASLD) is inviting members to apply, for their sites, to participate in the TARGET-LD Study which broadly studies outcomes for patients with chronic liver disease (CLD) and cirrhosis.

[Home](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/) Who We Are Who We Are AASLD is the leading organization of scientists and health care professionals committed to preventing and curing liver disease.

2022c2cf-372d-4603-94f3-abba9023fd26.text: We foster research that leads to improved treatment options for millions of liver disease patients. We advance the science and practice of hepatology through educational conferences, training programs, professional publications, and partnerships with government agencies and sister societies. Vision To Prevent and Cure Liver Disease Mission To advance and disseminate the science and practice of hepatology, and to promote liver health and quality patient care.

41f98a24-f6fd-4c5f-b085-babd43bcaa19.text: We advance the science and practice of hepatology through educational conferences, training programs, professional publications, and partnerships with government agencies and sister societies. Vision To Prevent and Cure Liver Disease Mission To advance and disseminate the science and practice of hepatology, and to promote liver health and quality patient care. AASLD's membership encompasses ALL professionals dedicated to hepatobiliary discoveries and patient care.

32d55b53-29af-4ece-bd08-30d8d3c10e6b.text: Vision To Prevent and Cure Liver Disease Mission To advance and disseminate the science and practice of hepatology, and to promote liver health and quality patient care. AASLD's membership encompasses ALL professionals dedicated to hepatobiliary discoveries and patient care. AASLD's core values include mentoring, the sharing of knowledge, and a dedication to professional growth and development.

2041ee8d-2169-467f-90f7-f26e84cfe82e.text: AASLD's membership encompasses ALL professionals dedicated to hepatobiliary discoveries and patient care. AASLD's core values include mentoring, the sharing of knowledge, and a dedication to professional growth and development. From complimentary journal subscriptions to no or reduced registration fees for meetings and educational events, membership in AASLD offers an opportunity to learn from, interact with, and remain in contact with leading professionals in the field.

dad09116-3d83-4a0d-9791-0403a3efb001.text: Each year, AASLD highlights opportunities for eligible members to engage in leadership roles within the association. Nominations are open until 11:59pm EDT on Sunday, March 9.

046b8102-7d7a-48f1-bf33-25c0ba6cfa76.text: [On This Page :](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-leadership-nominations#anchor-nav-1308680756) [2026 AASLD Open Leadership Positions](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-leadership-nominations#2026-aasld-open-leadership-positions) [AASLD Submission Site](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-leadership-nominations#aasld-submission-site) [Nominating Guidelines](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-leadership-nominations#nominating-guidelines) [Nomination Instructions](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-leadership-nominations#nomination-instructions) [Peer Nominations](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-leadership-nominations#peer-nominations) [Self-Nominations](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-leadership-nominations#self-nominations) 2026 AASLD Open Leadership Positions AASLD seeks diverse nominations for leadership roles including: Councilor, three-year term (Pathway to President) Councilor-at-Large, three-year term (1 opening) Treasurer, three-year term (1 opening) Nominating Committee, two-year term (2 openings) Ethics Committee Chair, three-year term (1 opening) Peer and self-nominations are accepted.

bb0a80d1-2bb6-451f-b9bd-205b4ead94c4.text: Self-nominations are welcomed encouraged. Terms begin January 1, 2026. The deadline to submit an application is 11:59pm EDT on Sunday, March 9. AASLD's Commitment to Diversity, Equity, & Inclusion (DEI) AASLD is committed to creating and supporting a diverse, equitable, and inclusive culture within the association and all aspects of the hepatology field. For this year's nomination ballot, we seek to have a diverse pool of candidates in terms of ethnicity, gender, and career level.

Nominating Guidelines All active AASLD Members can participate in the nomination process. Peer-nominations and self-nominations are accepted.

51c6eddd-626b-457a-b442-689311db78a9.text: Nominating Guidelines All active AASLD Members can participate in the nomination process. Peer-nominations and self-nominations are accepted. Both the nominator and the nominee must be AASLD members in good standing. Member Nominations Each member can submit up to three nominations for a leadership position. Please be aware that you will be required to complete a nomination application for each eligible position in the portal.

a9247ec1-7f12-48ab-834b-57d222e59e3f.text: Member Nominations Each member can submit up to three nominations for a leadership position. Please be aware that you will be required to complete a nomination application for each eligible position in the portal. Also, if you are a nominator, AASLD recommends nominating an individual within the first two weeks of the cycle opening to ensure the nominee has adequate time to complete the application.

0c77f19c-d664-4c90-81ff-27bf0522624f.text: Please be aware that you will be required to complete a nomination application for each eligible position in the portal. Nomination Instructions Peer Nominations All Nominations must be submitted online with the following information: The peer-nominator must submit the following information: Full name of the AASLD member nominee Leadership position for the nominee Nomination statement After the nominator submits the nomination application, the process is complete.

e6994152-622e-4a48-b35f-c061217bbb60.text: Also, if you are a nominator, AASLD recommends nominating an individual within the first two weeks of the cycle opening to ensure the nominee has adequate time to complete the application. [Leadership Position Descriptions](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-leadership-nominations#card-accordion-2028659812) [Governing Board](https://www.aasld.org/governing-board) : Councilor Councilors serve as members of the Governing Board and manage the affairs of the association, and review, establish, and modify professional scientific business administrative policies within the limits of the Bylaws to further the purposes of AASLD.

daabcefb-239e-4c5a-a1be-4681f678f769.text: Have your abridged Curriculum vitae (CV) ready, no more than 4 pages. [Leadership Position Descriptions](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-leadership-nominations#card-accordion-2028659812) [Governing Board](https://www.aasld.org/governing-board) : Councilor Councilors serve as members of the Governing Board and manage the affairs of the association, and review, establish, and modify professional scientific business administrative policies within the limits of the Bylaws to further the purposes of AASLD. Criteria Accomplishments in the study or care of liver diseases, in the fields of clinical or basic research.

2231b0cc-9a4d-4d4e-a977-c9b396590b30.text: [Leadership Position Descriptions](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-leadership-nominations#card-accordion-2028659812) [Governing Board](https://www.aasld.org/governing-board) : Councilor Councilors serve as members of the Governing Board and manage the affairs of the association, and review, establish, and modify professional scientific business administrative policies within the limits of the Bylaws to further the purposes of AASLD. Criteria Accomplishments in the study or care of liver diseases, in the fields of clinical or basic research. Evidence of commitment to AASLD activities, by presentations at meetings of the Association membership such as postgraduate course faculty, and service on AASLD committees.

0c33bab0-f85b-4b2b-b42e-2ce897bb938a.text: Criteria Accomplishments in the study or care of liver diseases, in the fields of clinical or basic research. Evidence of commitment to AASLD activities, by presentations at meetings of the Association membership such as postgraduate course faculty, and service on AASLD committees. Evidence of organizational and leadership roles in academic and/or non-academic organizational leadership roles. Previous Councilors are ineligible for re-election to the Governing Board.

854d102b-6336-4452-8baf-acc5b6c218f3.text: Evidence of organizational and leadership roles in academic and/or non-academic organizational leadership roles. Previous Councilors are ineligible for re-election to the Governing Board. Regular members who serve in a leadership capacity for related organizations or are employed full time in the pharmaceutical or biotech industries are ineligible for election to office. No two members of the Governing Board should be from the same institution.

41ba9a74-2612-43c1-967d-3efee36f73ca.text: Regular members who serve in a leadership capacity for related organizations or are employed full time in the pharmaceutical or biotech industries are ineligible for election to office. No two members of the Governing Board should be from the same institution. The composition of the Governing Board should reflect the diversity of the membership. The Councilor will be on the pathway to AASLD President.

a0bae4dc-038d-4e8a-ac88-a44bb96759ef.text: The composition of the Governing Board should reflect the diversity of the membership. The Councilor will be on the pathway to AASLD President. Specific Duties Manages the affairs of the association, and reviews, establishes, and modifies professional scientific business administrative policies within the limits of the Bylaws to further the purposes of AASLD. Establishes and reviews long-term objectives of the AASLD and determines the priority of all programs and activities.

4ae1c53a-dc4d-4049-90db-e5925f9537e8.text: Specific Duties Manages the affairs of the association, and reviews, establishes, and modifies professional scientific business administrative policies within the limits of the Bylaws to further the purposes of AASLD. Establishes and reviews long-term objectives of the AASLD and determines the priority of all programs and activities. Approves an annual budget, establishes financial goals for the Association, oversees the financial operations of the Association, and has discretion in the control, management of investments and disbursement of funds.

9d0a9c08-0dc5-4ef7-8ce3-5fa21349ccb5.text: Establishes and reviews long-term objectives of the AASLD and determines the priority of all programs and activities. Approves an annual budget, establishes financial goals for the Association, oversees the financial operations of the Association, and has discretion in the control, management of investments and disbursement of funds. [Governing Board](https://www.aasld.org/governing-board) : Councilor-at-Large Councilors-at-Large serve as members of the Governing Board with overlapping duties.

8154d0d7-196a-4a8b-afd2-1575229b7678.text: Approves an annual budget, establishes financial goals for the Association, oversees the financial operations of the Association, and has discretion in the control, management of investments and disbursement of funds. [Governing Board](https://www.aasld.org/governing-board) : Councilor-at-Large Councilors-at-Large serve as members of the Governing Board with overlapping duties. The Councilor-at-Large serves a three-year term.

9bc4b824-ef99-4da6-8237-366c99eb67a4.text: [Governing Board](https://www.aasld.org/governing-board) : Councilor-at-Large Councilors-at-Large serve as members of the Governing Board with overlapping duties. The Councilor-at-Large serves a three-year term. Criteria Accomplishments in the study or care of liver diseases, in the fields of clinical or basic research. Evidence of commitment to AASLD activities, by presentations at meetings of the Association membership and a commitment to service on AASLD committees.

c91ba1d5-6930-475b-9082-16e152807adb.text: Criteria Accomplishments in the study or care of liver diseases, in the fields of clinical or basic research. Evidence of commitment to AASLD activities, by presentations at meetings of the Association membership and a commitment to service on AASLD committees. Evidence of organizational and leadership roles in academic and/or non-academic organizational leadership roles. Ideally, the Councilor-at-Large should represent areas within the membership that are currently under-represented.

b1c01668-dda0-4e41-b727-ba29d2eaf161.text: Evidence of organizational and leadership roles in academic and/or non-academic organizational leadership roles. Ideally, the Councilor-at-Large should represent areas within the membership that are currently under-represented. Regular members who serve in a leadership capacity for related organizations or are employed full time in the pharmaceutical or biotech industries are ineligible for election to office. No two members of the Governing Board should be from the same institution.

Establishes and reviews long-term objectives of the AASLD and determines the priority of all programs and activities. Approves an annual budget, establishes financial goals for the Association, oversees the financial operations of the Association, and has discretion in the control, management of investments and disbursement of funds.

9fe3d4b3-ffca-487d-8ef2-d00607d1d4e8.text: Establishes and reviews long-term objectives of the AASLD and determines the priority of all programs and activities. Approves an annual budget, establishes financial goals for the Association, oversees the financial operations of the Association, and has discretion in the control, management of investments and disbursement of funds. [Governing Board](https://www.aasld.org/governing-board) : Treasurer The AASLD Treasurer serves as member of the Governing Board. The Treasurer serves a three-year term.

2cf310e5-9892-4099-9496-7b87e5b959b4.text: [Governing Board](https://www.aasld.org/governing-board) : Treasurer The AASLD Treasurer serves as member of the Governing Board. The Treasurer serves a three-year term. Criteria Evidence of major administrative and fiscal responsibilities and skills. Accomplishments in the study or care of liver diseases, in the fields of clinical or basic research.

db6e78f1-e66e-4efc-8235-c88e43bcf945.text: Criteria Evidence of major administrative and fiscal responsibilities and skills. Accomplishments in the study or care of liver diseases, in the fields of clinical or basic research. Evidence of commitment to AASLD activities, by presentations at meetings of the Association membership such as postgraduate course faculty, and service on AASLD committees. Evidence of organizational and leadership roles in academic and/or non-academic organizational leadership roles.

3a91a9cf-bca1-45a2-b750-4c0074b78723.text: Evidence of commitment to AASLD activities, by presentations at meetings of the Association membership such as postgraduate course faculty, and service on AASLD committees. Evidence of organizational and leadership roles in academic and/or non-academic organizational leadership roles. Regular members who serve in a leadership capacity for related organizations or are employed full time in the pharmaceutical or biotech industries are ineligible for election to office.

fce29cd6-90ba-4226-8f40-47927ddc513b.text: Evidence of organizational and leadership roles in academic and/or non-academic organizational leadership roles. Regular members who serve in a leadership capacity for related organizations or are employed full time in the pharmaceutical or biotech industries are ineligible for election to office. [Nominating Committee](https://www.aasld.org/member-committees/nominating-committee) The Nominating Committee reviews nominations for leadership positions for the Governing Board and Chair of the Ethics Committee and makes preliminary standing committee appointments.

d979b0cf-59bd-4949-9c6a-a7f06c77646a.text: Regular members who serve in a leadership capacity for related organizations or are employed full time in the pharmaceutical or biotech industries are ineligible for election to office. [Nominating Committee](https://www.aasld.org/member-committees/nominating-committee) The Nominating Committee reviews nominations for leadership positions for the Governing Board and Chair of the Ethics Committee and makes preliminary standing committee appointments. The President-Elect finalizes these appointments and recommends their approval by the Governing Board.

bf024b61-2003-4ed9-924d-853dff042ae9.text: [Nominating Committee](https://www.aasld.org/member-committees/nominating-committee) The Nominating Committee reviews nominations for leadership positions for the Governing Board and Chair of the Ethics Committee and makes preliminary standing committee appointments. The President-Elect finalizes these appointments and recommends their approval by the Governing Board. Nominating Committee members serve a two-year term. The Chair is the Governing Board Past-President and serves a one-year term.

4c509e2c-8f41-4131-912d-fe3793ae03e2.text: Nominating Committee members serve a two-year term. The Chair is the Governing Board Past-President and serves a one-year term. Criteria Accomplishments in the study or care of patients with liver diseases, and in the fields of clinical, basic or health services research. Evidence of commitment to AASLD activities such as presentations at meetings of the Association and a commitment to service on AASLD committees. No two members of the slate for Nominating Committee should be from the same institution.

ba9f60ba-f64b-42a0-b155-a4094892f04b.text: Evidence of commitment to AASLD activities such as presentations at meetings of the Association and a commitment to service on AASLD committees. No two members of the slate for Nominating Committee should be from the same institution. Service on the Nominating Committee should be limited to once every five years. Candidates who appeared on the ballot who were not elected, are eligible for nomination in the next two subsequent years without reapplying. Amended (GB05-20-14-10).

d69571a2-5b21-4680-a648-cfe54a390548.text: Candidates who appeared on the ballot who were not elected, are eligible for nomination in the next two subsequent years without reapplying. Amended (GB05-20-14-10). Sitting members of the Nominating Committee are ineligible for nomination for any elected leadership position during their term. Regular members who serve in a leadership capacity for related organizations or are employed full time in the pharmaceutical or biotech industries are ineligible for election to office.

0c0c8b8d-6881-4ee3-95ce-93be3a9d9535.text: Sitting members of the Nominating Committee are ineligible for nomination for any elected leadership position during their term. Regular members who serve in a leadership capacity for related organizations or are employed full time in the pharmaceutical or biotech industries are ineligible for election to office. Specific Duties Annually develops a slate of: Three (3) candidates for the Councilor position on the Governing Board. Three (3) candidates for Councilor-at-Large.

9de98518-ebf8-49b2-96ab-5b5196a922c3.text: Specific Duties Annually develops a slate of: Three (3) candidates for the Councilor position on the Governing Board. Three (3) candidates for Councilor-at-Large. Ten (10) candidates for the Nominating Committee.

cf7908ea-eb0e-407c-9a6a-484fdc973c92.text: Three (3) candidates for Councilor-at-Large. Ten (10) candidates for the Nominating Committee. Every three years, develops a slate of three (3) candidates for: Secretary and/or Treasurer Chair of the Ethics Committee Chair of [Ethics Committee](https://www.aasld.org/member-committees/ethics-committee) The purpose of the committee is to serve in an advisory capacity to the Governing Board in the evaluation of ethical issues relating to the activities of AASLD, and its members acting in the name of AASLD.

03989752-da6a-4ad7-87b5-8d993b7d03c5.text: Ten (10) candidates for the Nominating Committee. AASLD Constitution ARTICLE I Name The name of the Corporation is the American Association for the Study of Liver Diseases. ARTICLE II The American Association for the Study of Liver Diseases, henceforth termed the Association, is incorporated under the General Not-For-Profit Corporation Act of the State of Illinois.

d40de578-4894-4c5b-8986-ffa287774656.text: AASLD Constitution ARTICLE I Name The name of the Corporation is the American Association for the Study of Liver Diseases. ARTICLE II The American Association for the Study of Liver Diseases, henceforth termed the Association, is incorporated under the General Not-For-Profit Corporation Act of the State of Illinois. The Association was incorporated on March 23, 1950.

32ddc3c0-7b35-4f2f-a89a-a438f311b7e2.text: ARTICLE II The American Association for the Study of Liver Diseases, henceforth termed the Association, is incorporated under the General Not-For-Profit Corporation Act of the State of Illinois. The Association was incorporated on March 23, 1950. ARTICLE III Objectives and Purposes [Section I](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-constitution-and-bylaws#card-accordion-42651743) To foster the development, dissemination, and application of new knowledge about liver and biliary tract, and their diseases; to provide leadership and aid in medical education, research, and delivery of health care in this field.

d2880443-58c0-4644-b023-e10352834374.text: The Association was incorporated on March 23, 1950. ARTICLE III Objectives and Purposes [Section I](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-constitution-and-bylaws#card-accordion-42651743) To foster the development, dissemination, and application of new knowledge about liver and biliary tract, and their diseases; to provide leadership and aid in medical education, research, and delivery of health care in this field. [Section II](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-constitution-and-bylaws#card-accordion-1030292741) To accomplish the purposes outlined in Section I by convening meetings for and otherwise communicating with members of the Association and others; to solicit and receive dues and donations; to receive, manage and invest real and personal property, money and securities; to disburse money and assets for activities in keeping with the stated purposes.

d35ba9da-28d5-4987-a766-12511aa189ec.text: ARTICLE III Objectives and Purposes [Section I](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-constitution-and-bylaws#card-accordion-42651743) To foster the development, dissemination, and application of new knowledge about liver and biliary tract, and their diseases; to provide leadership and aid in medical education, research, and delivery of health care in this field. [Section II](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-constitution-and-bylaws#card-accordion-1030292741) To accomplish the purposes outlined in Section I by convening meetings for and otherwise communicating with members of the Association and others; to solicit and receive dues and donations; to receive, manage and invest real and personal property, money and securities; to disburse money and assets for activities in keeping with the stated purposes. [Section III](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-constitution-and-bylaws#card-accordion-1772414840) The purpose for which the Association is organized is the advancement of medical education, research and clinical care in the field of liver and biliary tract.

8206d3d7-9c8b-4b7d-b05b-cae52554fdca.text: [Section II](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-constitution-and-bylaws#card-accordion-1030292741) To accomplish the purposes outlined in Section I by convening meetings for and otherwise communicating with members of the Association and others; to solicit and receive dues and donations; to receive, manage and invest real and personal property, money and securities; to disburse money and assets for activities in keeping with the stated purposes. [Section III](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-constitution-and-bylaws#card-accordion-1772414840) The purpose for which the Association is organized is the advancement of medical education, research and clinical care in the field of liver and biliary tract. The purpose is exclusively educational, scientific, and charitable.

1511e74c-6c6c-4683-83ea-5b162d4903d8.text: [Section III](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/aasld-constitution-and-bylaws#card-accordion-1772414840) The purpose for which the Association is organized is the advancement of medical education, research and clinical care in the field of liver and biliary tract. The purpose is exclusively educational, scientific, and charitable. Any net earnings of the Association or any of its activities shall be devoted exclusively to such purposes, and shall not inure to the benefit of any individual.

210bc5c3-e2c4-43fe-a44a-e2fd9dc9702d.text: The purpose is exclusively educational, scientific, and charitable.
