f8830be3-d47f-41fb-9753-75dc4ea03447.text: TITLE V Special Interest Groups The Governing Board may establish special interest groups (SIG) of members with shared interests in specialized areas of hepatology and related disciplines to foster collaboration and provide forums for networking among scientists and health care professionals. The Governing Board will appoint the initial SIG chair who will appoint an interim Steering Committee. Subsequent chairs and Steering Committee members will be elected by SIG members.

be0982f4-7cc4-4e8c-b795-13daa9ca5e28.text: The Governing Board will appoint the initial SIG chair who will appoint an interim Steering Committee. Subsequent chairs and Steering Committee members will be elected by SIG members. TITLE VI Dues The amount of annual dues shall be determined by the Governing Board, subject to approval by the membership. The Governing Board, at its discretion may waive or reduce any amount of dues.

cf1d288e-2743-4ed2-bc2e-93d8cea5ff77.text: TITLE VI Dues The amount of annual dues shall be determined by the Governing Board, subject to approval by the membership. The Governing Board, at its discretion may waive or reduce any amount of dues. TITLE VII Suspension and Resignation of Members Any member of the Association may be suspended for nonpayment of dues or for unprofessional conduct. A member whose dues are sixty (60) days in arrears shall be so notified, and the member shall be suspended.

85bb664b-ca88-49f5-ab5b-ed3cd116adea.text: TITLE VII Suspension and Resignation of Members Any member of the Association may be suspended for nonpayment of dues or for unprofessional conduct. A member whose dues are sixty (60) days in arrears shall be so notified, and the member shall be suspended. A member suspended for nonpayment of dues may be reinstated upon payment of dues for the current year in which they are reinstated, as well any dues in arrears for the immediately prior year.

618507b4-df81-4c3a-ac2f-3e0f9e9966dd.text: A member whose dues are sixty (60) days in arrears shall be so notified, and the member shall be suspended. A member suspended for nonpayment of dues may be reinstated upon payment of dues for the current year in which they are reinstated, as well any dues in arrears for the immediately prior year. A member accused of unprofessional conduct must be informed of the charges, and shall have the right to be heard by the Governing Board in his or her own defense.

ec4c8d79-a7ca-480b-9fe4-0e5f121ef0c1.text: A member suspended for nonpayment of dues may be reinstated upon payment of dues for the current year in which they are reinstated, as well any dues in arrears for the immediately prior year. A member accused of unprofessional conduct must be informed of the charges, and shall have the right to be heard by the Governing Board in his or her own defense. Expulsion for unprofessional conduct shall require approval of the Governing Board.

0afbc306-7ab9-476c-a622-ff4d986bb4b6.text: A member accused of unprofessional conduct must be informed of the charges, and shall have the right to be heard by the Governing Board in his or her own defense. Expulsion for unprofessional conduct shall require approval of the Governing Board. A member may resign at any time by filing his or her written resignation with the Secretary, but such resignation shall not exempt him or her from payment of dues owed, including dues for the terminal year.

f860cd02-7179-4e7c-9955-a4aaf3c434cc.text: Expulsion for unprofessional conduct shall require approval of the Governing Board. A member may resign at any time by filing his or her written resignation with the Secretary, but such resignation shall not exempt him or her from payment of dues owed, including dues for the terminal year. TITLE VIII Annual Business Meeting The annual business meeting of the Association shall be held at a time and place to be determined by the Governing Board.

592500bc-95fd-43c4-9d1a-eec886637063.text: A member may resign at any time by filing his or her written resignation with the Secretary, but such resignation shall not exempt him or her from payment of dues owed, including dues for the terminal year. TITLE VIII Annual Business Meeting The annual business meeting of the Association shall be held at a time and place to be determined by the Governing Board. Not less than thirty days notice in writing by mail shall be given by the Secretary to each member, stating the time and place fixed for the holding of each such annual meeting.

2fb70dbb-124b-413a-a2da-cf99b3831d32.text: TITLE VIII Annual Business Meeting The annual business meeting of the Association shall be held at a time and place to be determined by the Governing Board. Not less than thirty days notice in writing by mail shall be given by the Secretary to each member, stating the time and place fixed for the holding of each such annual meeting. A quorum shall consist of at least fifty (50) current Regular or Emeritus members.

8340386c-0c80-4b9e-a563-f6936598617a.text: Not less than thirty days notice in writing by mail shall be given by the Secretary to each member, stating the time and place fixed for the holding of each such annual meeting. A quorum shall consist of at least fifty (50) current Regular or Emeritus members. Voting may take place by show of hands or written ballot; the Secretary and the President-Elect shall supervise voting procedures and the counting of votes.

b0ac1345-b568-48eb-b5a8-3abdae6bf86b.text: A quorum shall consist of at least fifty (50) current Regular or Emeritus members. Voting may take place by show of hands or written ballot; the Secretary and the President-Elect shall supervise voting procedures and the counting of votes. The current edition of Robert’s Rules of Order shall guide procedures at annual meetings of the Association, and of the Governing Board. TITLE IX Amendments The Articles of Incorporation, Constitution and Bylaws of the Association may be amended by ballot.

77a4ce40-4e69-4be2-ae96-c8702b10b73e.text: The current edition of Robert’s Rules of Order shall guide procedures at annual meetings of the Association, and of the Governing Board. TITLE IX Amendments The Articles of Incorporation, Constitution and Bylaws of the Association may be amended by ballot. At least fifty (50) ballots must be received, and approval of two-thirds of the members voting shall be required for an amendment to be adopted. The balloting period shall be at least twenty days. Have Questions?

a35dc56c-2808-4fcb-96d1-70e0436cc613.text: The balloting period shall be at least twenty days. Have Questions? 082a2779-0c9b-4ac2-845d-163e84db27a5.text: AASLD’s code for the assessment and management of conflict of interest is also available in [PDF format](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/sites/default/files/2022-05/Revised%20Code%20for%20COI%20-%20June%202021%20Update%20new.pdf) . Purpose And Application Complex relationships with for-profit and not-for-profit organizations and entities may by their existence present a perceived or real conflict with the missions and values of the AASLD.
082a2779-0c9b-4ac2-845d-163e84db27a5.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
082a2779-0c9b-4ac2-845d-163e84db27a5.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

b6c6edf9-d513-48dd-afd7-b630814e0d9b.text: In as much as the existence of real or perceived conflicts of interest serves to undermine the stature, integrity, creditability and function of the AASLD, AASLD must insure that its membership, leadership and the public-at-large understand the importance it places on identification and resolution of conflicts. The independence and the credibility of AASLD require implementation of a clear policy that can be enacted in a practical, fair and transparent manner.
b6c6edf9-d513-48dd-afd7-b630814e0d9b.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
b6c6edf9-d513-48dd-afd7-b630814e0d9b.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

e2ebced4-bdbe-4616-b958-f5cd8343cfc7.text: Conflicts of interest are defined as any circumstances that create a risk that professional judgments or actions regarding a primary interest will be unduly influenced by a secondary interest. 1 Primary interests are those associated with the stated mission of the AASLD. Secondary interests may be financial or non-financial in nature (e.g. intellectual conflict of interest,2 membership in other organizations, or institutional or corporate associations).
e2ebced4-bdbe-4616-b958-f5cd8343cfc7.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
e2ebced4-bdbe-4616-b958-f5cd8343cfc7.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

47bbd4e2-4caa-4d62-b26b-9995d2b4c4c9.text: Secondary interests may be financial or non-financial in nature (e.g. intellectual conflict of interest,2 membership in other organizations, or institutional or corporate associations). Application of this policy is to be temporally phased in with the addition of new members and reappointments. Further, the degree of permitted associations will be regulated in a fashion that is commensurate with the influence that the individual has in the creation, implementation and/or execution of AASLD functions.
47bbd4e2-4caa-4d62-b26b-9995d2b4c4c9.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
47bbd4e2-4caa-4d62-b26b-9995d2b4c4c9.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

100a7fa4-4bfc-4c8e-8bba-ea124572468f.text: Application of this policy is to be temporally phased in with the addition of new members and reappointments. Further, the degree of permitted associations will be regulated in a fashion that is commensurate with the influence that the individual has in the creation, implementation and/or execution of AASLD functions. Since the AASLD plays roles of advocacy for clinical care, education and research in liver diseases, the policy shall cover all individuals who participate at any level in those functions.
100a7fa4-4bfc-4c8e-8bba-ea124572468f.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
100a7fa4-4bfc-4c8e-8bba-ea124572468f.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

7ae1b178-4cff-4ece-89ae-0df768cdea12.text: Further, the degree of permitted associations will be regulated in a fashion that is commensurate with the influence that the individual has in the creation, implementation and/or execution of AASLD functions. Since the AASLD plays roles of advocacy for clinical care, education and research in liver diseases, the policy shall cover all individuals who participate at any level in those functions. The definition of conflict, review of disclosures and the adjudication and resolution of conflicts requires a multi-layer effort that is charged to the Ethics committee.

78af142a-8604-4c0d-9e90-9cadcb18d327.text: Since the AASLD plays roles of advocacy for clinical care, education and research in liver diseases, the policy shall cover all individuals who participate at any level in those functions. The definition of conflict, review of disclosures and the adjudication and resolution of conflicts requires a multi-layer effort that is charged to the Ethics committee. The policy outlined will require diligent interpretation in complex cases, with the goal of providing clarity for members as they engage in their functions.
78af142a-8604-4c0d-9e90-9cadcb18d327.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
78af142a-8604-4c0d-9e90-9cadcb18d327.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

b9072d27-8645-4ca0-b200-38d7c182b3ba.text: The definition of conflict, review of disclosures and the adjudication and resolution of conflicts requires a multi-layer effort that is charged to the Ethics committee. The policy outlined will require diligent interpretation in complex cases, with the goal of providing clarity for members as they engage in their functions. The overriding goal is to provide objective conflict management. The application of this Code is not intended to be punitive to the member.
b9072d27-8645-4ca0-b200-38d7c182b3ba.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
b9072d27-8645-4ca0-b200-38d7c182b3ba.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

0c9a834b-5a15-4c02-9467-7e4110b9e335.text: Definitions The following terms are defined for purposes of this Code: Company: A Company is an entity that develops, produces, markets, or distributes drugs, devices, services or therapies used to diagnose, treat, monitor, manage, and alleviate health conditions and whose interests could reasonably be seen to overlap with the interests, missions and values of the AASLD. This definition is not intended to include entities outside of the health care sector, or entities through which physicians provide clinical services directly to patients. Direct Financial Relationship: A Direct Financial Relationship is a compensated relationship with a Company held by an individual that should generate an IRS Form W-2, 1099 or equivalent income report.

Direct Financial Relationship: A Direct Financial Relationship is a compensated relationship with a Company held by an individual that should generate an IRS Form W-2, 1099 or equivalent income report. All direct financial relationships that are in effect, or had been in effect, any time within the 12-month period preceding the date of disclosure must be reported.

AASLD will separate their efforts to seek Educational Grants, Corporate Sponsorships, Charitable Contributions, and support for Research Grants from their programmatic decisions. 24ccb186-71ca-4b4b-9fd7-7d4dde226687.text: Once AASLD determines that a program is needed, it is permissible to assess the availability of funds. A. The Ethics Committee of AASLD will be responsible for evaluating and adjudicating conflicts of interest and guiding the interactions of AASLD, and its Members, and monitoring the compliance with this Code. (See Appendix C). B. Approval of a motion involving identification or management of a conflict of interest by the Ethics Committee requires a two-thirds majority of voting members.

Approval of a motion involving identification or management of a conflict of interest by the Ethics Committee requires a two-thirds majority of voting members. C. The President and President-elect, Chair of the Ethics Committee, and Chairs and Chairs-elect of the CME, Practice Guidelines and Practice Metrics Committees may not have Direct Financial Relationships with Companies during his or her term of service.

C. The President and President-elect, Chair of the Ethics Committee, and Chairs and Chairs-elect of the CME, Practice Guidelines and Practice Metrics Committees may not have Direct Financial Relationships with Companies during his or her term of service. AASLD will require that a majority (based on simple numerical majority) of CME and PMC Committee members are free of conflicts of interest.

9d47e633-d3d1-4df2-a4bf-da2f2f28daeb.text: C. The President and President-elect, Chair of the Ethics Committee, and Chairs and Chairs-elect of the CME, Practice Guidelines and Practice Metrics Committees may not have Direct Financial Relationships with Companies during his or her term of service. AASLD will require that a majority (based on simple numerical majority) of CME and PMC Committee members are free of conflicts of interest. D. Other AASLD Governing Board members, Committee Chairs (except Practice Guidelines, Practice Metrics, and CME Committees), SIG Chairs, Committee members, a minority of guidance writing group members, and Editors-in-Chief of AASLD journals are permitted to have Direct Financial Relationships with Companies up to $10,000 per company per year; and up to $50,000 total compensation per year received for participation in Scientific Consultancy/Advisorship, and Data Safety Monitoring Boards (except writing group members).

1ee4c186-dbdb-4205-9745-4d8c188ebfd1.text: AASLD will require that a majority (based on simple numerical majority) of CME and PMC Committee members are free of conflicts of interest. D. Other AASLD Governing Board members, Committee Chairs (except Practice Guidelines, Practice Metrics, and CME Committees), SIG Chairs, Committee members, a minority of guidance writing group members, and Editors-in-Chief of AASLD journals are permitted to have Direct Financial Relationships with Companies up to $10,000 per company per year; and up to $50,000 total compensation per year received for participation in Scientific Consultancy/Advisorship, and Data Safety Monitoring Boards (except writing group members). Effective January 2021, such relationships and the dollar amount of the compensation from each company must be disclosed.
1ee4c186-dbdb-4205-9745-4d8c188ebfd1.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

a645eff7-1516-425a-adde-1116f1a46b72.text: D. Other AASLD Governing Board members, Committee Chairs (except Practice Guidelines, Practice Metrics, and CME Committees), SIG Chairs, Committee members, a minority of guidance writing group members, and Editors-in-Chief of AASLD journals are permitted to have Direct Financial Relationships with Companies up to $10,000 per company per year; and up to $50,000 total compensation per year received for participation in Scientific Consultancy/Advisorship, and Data Safety Monitoring Boards (except writing group members). Effective January 2021, such relationships and the dollar amount of the compensation from each company must be disclosed. Journal Editors are required to recuse themselves from involvement with any manuscript funded by or impacting companies with which they have personal financial relationships.

7f5604f1-5caa-40c2-93d0-96d719317f82.text: Effective January 2021, such relationships and the dollar amount of the compensation from each company must be disclosed. Journal Editors are required to recuse themselves from involvement with any manuscript funded by or impacting companies with which they have personal financial relationships. These manuscripts should be assigned to and handled by a non-conflicted Deputy or Associate Editor, with no input from the Editor. These recusals must be documented and tracked.

4429a367-62f8-4e4c-a917-14e7aec288fa.text: G. The President and President-elect, and Chair of the Ethics Committee and Chairs and Chairs- elect of the CME and Practice Guidelines Committees may provide uncompensated service to Companies and accept reasonable travel reimbursement in connection with those services. The President and President-elect and Chair of the Ethics Committee and Chairs and Chairs-elect of the CME, Practice Guidelines and Practice Metrics Committee may accept research support as long as grant money is paid to the institution (e.g. academic medical center) where the research is conducted, not to the individual or to a practice.
4429a367-62f8-4e4c-a917-14e7aec288fa.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
4429a367-62f8-4e4c-a917-14e7aec288fa.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

0c471ec4-2c22-426c-b400-9c0ec452461d.text: The President and President-elect and Chair of the Ethics Committee and Chairs and Chairs-elect of the CME, Practice Guidelines and Practice Metrics Committee may accept research support as long as grant money is paid to the institution (e.g. academic medical center) where the research is conducted, not to the individual or to a practice. Research support, uncompensated services and other permitted relationships must be disclosed to the AASLD, regardless of any monetary value or its equivalent.

AASLD does not consider research funds provided to a member’s institution as a conflict of interest. Membership as an officer or member of the governing board of a related professional association and appointment as editor or associate editor of a competing journal are not allowed.

Committee Chairs and members are required to recuse themselves from decisions that could result in direct/indirect financial benefit or could reasonably be perceived as a conflict of interest, even if these activities have not been specifically precluded by AASLD COI policy. 2. Transparency AASLD will make their conflict of interest policies available to their members and the public. AASLD, through the Ethics Committee, will manage conflicts of interest in a variety of ways.
2f8b7420-1483-4ea8-9a9b-ef5d405cf15c.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
2f8b7420-1483-4ea8-9a9b-ef5d405cf15c.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

e0432636-989d-4c20-9c88-7d4d0defca25.text: Transparency AASLD will make their conflict of interest policies available to their members and the public. AASLD, through the Ethics Committee, will manage conflicts of interest in a variety of ways. This may include, but is not limited to, disclosure, recusal, divestiture, or AASLD-independent review. The Ethics Committee will select conflict of interest management mechanisms that are appropriate for the activity, type of relationship and role of the individual under consideration (see Appendix C).
e0432636-989d-4c20-9c88-7d4d0defca25.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
e0432636-989d-4c20-9c88-7d4d0defca25.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

944f4ee0-0e4c-49d7-874d-3efd931892a3.text: This may include, but is not limited to, disclosure, recusal, divestiture, or AASLD-independent review. The Ethics Committee will select conflict of interest management mechanisms that are appropriate for the activity, type of relationship and role of the individual under consideration (see Appendix C). A. AASLD, through the Ethics Committee, will provide written disclosure forms to individuals who serve on behalf of the AASLD (see Appendix D), and will use the disclosed information to manage conflicts of interest in decision-making.

A. AASLD, through the Ethics Committee, will provide written disclosure forms to individuals who serve on behalf of the AASLD (see Appendix D), and will use the disclosed information to manage conflicts of interest in decision-making. AASLD will require volunteers to update disclosure information at least annually and whenever material changes occur.

AASLD, through the Ethics Committee, will provide written disclosure forms to individuals who serve on behalf of the AASLD (see Appendix D), and will use the disclosed information to manage conflicts of interest in decision-making. AASLD will require volunteers to update disclosure information at least annually and whenever material changes occur. B. AASLD will disclose all Direct Financial Relationships and uncompensated relationships held by the President and President-elect making this information available to their members and the public.

AASLD will disclose all Direct Financial Relationships and uncompensated relationships held by the President and President-elect making this information available to their members and the public. C. Disclosure forms obtained during any nominating process shall not be included as part of the review of the candidate by the Nominating Committee.

Disclosure forms obtained during any nominating process shall not be included as part of the review of the candidate by the Nominating Committee. The disclosures of the finalists selected by the Nominating Committee shall be reviewed by the Ethics Committee prior to review by the Governing Board. 3.

Clinical Practice Guidelines / Guidances and Quality Measures AASLD will base Clinical Practice Guidelines/Guidances and Quality Measures on scientific evidence and will follow a transparent Guideline/Guidance and Quality Measure development process that is not subject to Company or other external influence.
5ee357b8-fb65-45da-bd71-a15bd5528898.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
5ee357b8-fb65-45da-bd71-a15bd5528898.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

9a2edfd3-c1b7-43f3-879f-23e2c3bb0e72.text: 3. Clinical Practice Guidelines / Guidances and Quality Measures AASLD will base Clinical Practice Guidelines/Guidances and Quality Measures on scientific evidence and will follow a transparent Guideline/Guidance and Quality Measure development process that is not subject to Company or other external influence. AASLD will publish a description of their Guideline/Guidance and Quality Measures development processes, including their process for identifying and managing conflicts of interest on AASLD websites and in AASLD journals.
9a2edfd3-c1b7-43f3-879f-23e2c3bb0e72.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
9a2edfd3-c1b7-43f3-879f-23e2c3bb0e72.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

b0aa10f7-0612-492a-9398-0a352c5aaa08.text: Clinical Practice Guidelines / Guidances and Quality Measures AASLD will base Clinical Practice Guidelines/Guidances and Quality Measures on scientific evidence and will follow a transparent Guideline/Guidance and Quality Measure development process that is not subject to Company or other external influence. AASLD will publish a description of their Guideline/Guidance and Quality Measures development processes, including their process for identifying and managing conflicts of interest on AASLD websites and in AASLD journals. A. AASLD will not permit direct Company support of the development of Clinical Practice Guidelines/Guidances, Guideline/Guidance Updates and Quality Measures.
b0aa10f7-0612-492a-9398-0a352c5aaa08.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
b0aa10f7-0612-492a-9398-0a352c5aaa08.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

5c9a866e-01c8-4a7f-8f7f-869bf2e32bde.text: AASLD will publish a description of their Guideline/Guidance and Quality Measures development processes, including their process for identifying and managing conflicts of interest on AASLD websites and in AASLD journals. A. AASLD will not permit direct Company support of the development of Clinical Practice Guidelines/Guidances, Guideline/Guidance Updates and Quality Measures. B.
5c9a866e-01c8-4a7f-8f7f-869bf2e32bde.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
5c9a866e-01c8-4a7f-8f7f-869bf2e32bde.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

903a4f7c-5cdf-43a3-9a83-d63c77ced597.text: A. AASLD will not permit direct Company support of the development of Clinical Practice Guidelines/Guidances, Guideline/Guidance Updates and Quality Measures. B. Permitted relationships and their disclosure for Clinical Practice Guidelines/Guidances and Quality Measures Committee members and Chairs are outlined in Appendix A. C. AASLD will require that a majority (based on simple numerical majority) of Guideline/Guidance or Quality Measures development writing panel/group members are free of conflicts of interest relevant to the subject matter of the Guideline/Guidance or Quality Measures during the period of Guideline/Guidance or and Quality Measures development.
903a4f7c-5cdf-43a3-9a83-d63c77ced597.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
903a4f7c-5cdf-43a3-9a83-d63c77ced597.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

61a40872-1c65-4036-abef-a54a61a9ceb5.text: B. Permitted relationships and their disclosure for Clinical Practice Guidelines/Guidances and Quality Measures Committee members and Chairs are outlined in Appendix A. C. AASLD will require that a majority (based on simple numerical majority) of Guideline/Guidance or Quality Measures development writing panel/group members are free of conflicts of interest relevant to the subject matter of the Guideline/Guidance or Quality Measures during the period of Guideline/Guidance or and Quality Measures development. Writing Panel/Group members without conflicts will be expected to remain conflict-free during their term of service on the Writing Panel/Group.
61a40872-1c65-4036-abef-a54a61a9ceb5.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
61a40872-1c65-4036-abef-a54a61a9ceb5.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

4c5140a0-5b33-40e3-92ce-54d86756bd74.text: Permitted relationships and their disclosure for Clinical Practice Guidelines/Guidances and Quality Measures Committee members and Chairs are outlined in Appendix A. C. AASLD will require that a majority (based on simple numerical majority) of Guideline/Guidance or Quality Measures development writing panel/group members are free of conflicts of interest relevant to the subject matter of the Guideline/Guidance or Quality Measures during the period of Guideline/Guidance or and Quality Measures development. Writing Panel/Group members without conflicts will be expected to remain conflict-free during their term of service on the Writing Panel/Group. For the minority of writing panel/group members who are not free of conflicts, AASLD will apply procedures for conflict of interest management developed in accordance with Section 2.
4c5140a0-5b33-40e3-92ce-54d86756bd74.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
4c5140a0-5b33-40e3-92ce-54d86756bd74.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

e7a4628c-b79b-48b1-aaab-46fb8ec73c01.text: Writing Panel/Group members without conflicts will be expected to remain conflict-free during their term of service on the Writing Panel/Group. For the minority of writing panel/group members who are not free of conflicts, AASLD will apply procedures for conflict of interest management developed in accordance with Section 2. As soon as a violation of COI policy is identified, the specific Committee or Writing Panel/Group member who was identified to be in violation would immediately lose voting and authorship privileges.
e7a4628c-b79b-48b1-aaab-46fb8ec73c01.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
e7a4628c-b79b-48b1-aaab-46fb8ec73c01.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

459c6d25-ff96-43a2-8c6e-c1d526a8b167.text: For the minority of writing panel/group members who are not free of conflicts, AASLD will apply procedures for conflict of interest management developed in accordance with Section 2. As soon as a violation of COI policy is identified, the specific Committee or Writing Panel/Group member who was identified to be in violation would immediately lose voting and authorship privileges. These privileges can be reinstated after the conflict is rectified in a satisfactory manner, as adjudicated by the Ethics Committee.
459c6d25-ff96-43a2-8c6e-c1d526a8b167.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
459c6d25-ff96-43a2-8c6e-c1d526a8b167.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

7afdeb21-7dae-4dc6-9a5b-ebdde11f3d2b.text: As soon as a violation of COI policy is identified, the specific Committee or Writing Panel/Group member who was identified to be in violation would immediately lose voting and authorship privileges. These privileges can be reinstated after the conflict is rectified in a satisfactory manner, as adjudicated by the Ethics Committee. D. AASLD will require the writing panel/group chair(s) to be free of conflicts of interest and to remain free of conflicts of interest during Guideline/Guidance or Quality Measures development up to the time of publication of the Guideline/Guidance or Quality Measures.
7afdeb21-7dae-4dc6-9a5b-ebdde11f3d2b.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
7afdeb21-7dae-4dc6-9a5b-ebdde11f3d2b.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

384bcbdc-a5bc-43a7-b532-5369c7bbbbb6.text: These privileges can be reinstated after the conflict is rectified in a satisfactory manner, as adjudicated by the Ethics Committee. D. AASLD will require the writing panel/group chair(s) to be free of conflicts of interest and to remain free of conflicts of interest during Guideline/Guidance or Quality Measures development up to the time of publication of the Guideline/Guidance or Quality Measures. E. Clinical Practice Guidelines/Guidances and Quality Measures Committee members and Chairs, as well as assigned Administrative Staff are expected to possess a sound knowledge of COI requirements for their respective Committees/Groups and to promptly report changes in COI.
384bcbdc-a5bc-43a7-b532-5369c7bbbbb6.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
384bcbdc-a5bc-43a7-b532-5369c7bbbbb6.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

28444281-0938-4d9c-a5e4-6bb50885c2a6.text: D. AASLD will require the writing panel/group chair(s) to be free of conflicts of interest and to remain free of conflicts of interest during Guideline/Guidance or Quality Measures development up to the time of publication of the Guideline/Guidance or Quality Measures. E. Clinical Practice Guidelines/Guidances and Quality Measures Committee members and Chairs, as well as assigned Administrative Staff are expected to possess a sound knowledge of COI requirements for their respective Committees/Groups and to promptly report changes in COI. Once the composition of a Committee/Writing Group has been reviewed by the Ethics Committee and approved by the Governing Board, the AASLD administrator assigned to each committee/group will be responsible for monitoring new conflicts and bringing any non-permitted conflict to the attention of the relevant Committee Chair and to the Ethics Committee.
28444281-0938-4d9c-a5e4-6bb50885c2a6.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
28444281-0938-4d9c-a5e4-6bb50885c2a6.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

580d2d9a-73ab-49e6-93fe-4108b300d79a.text: E. Clinical Practice Guidelines/Guidances and Quality Measures Committee members and Chairs, as well as assigned Administrative Staff are expected to possess a sound knowledge of COI requirements for their respective Committees/Groups and to promptly report changes in COI. Once the composition of a Committee/Writing Group has been reviewed by the Ethics Committee and approved by the Governing Board, the AASLD administrator assigned to each committee/group will be responsible for monitoring new conflicts and bringing any non-permitted conflict to the attention of the relevant Committee Chair and to the Ethics Committee. This specifically includes the reporting of any new conflict submitted by a non-conflicted Writing Group member that would cause the Writing Group to exceed the 50% threshold for non-conflicted members.
580d2d9a-73ab-49e6-93fe-4108b300d79a.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
580d2d9a-73ab-49e6-93fe-4108b300d79a.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

e839242a-7e3d-47e6-a417-ce13dafb7a56.text: Once the composition of a Committee/Writing Group has been reviewed by the Ethics Committee and approved by the Governing Board, the AASLD administrator assigned to each committee/group will be responsible for monitoring new conflicts and bringing any non-permitted conflict to the attention of the relevant Committee Chair and to the Ethics Committee. This specifically includes the reporting of any new conflict submitted by a non-conflicted Writing Group member that would cause the Writing Group to exceed the 50% threshold for non-conflicted members. F. AASLD will require that Guideline recommendations, Guidance statements or Quality Measures be subject to multiple levels of review, including rigorous peer-review by a range of experts.
e839242a-7e3d-47e6-a417-ce13dafb7a56.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
e839242a-7e3d-47e6-a417-ce13dafb7a56.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

6fe37c80-9b19-43d3-b12e-0c98fc1d63d9.text: This specifically includes the reporting of any new conflict submitted by a non-conflicted Writing Group member that would cause the Writing Group to exceed the 50% threshold for non-conflicted members. F. AASLD will require that Guideline recommendations, Guidance statements or Quality Measures be subject to multiple levels of review, including rigorous peer-review by a range of experts. AASLD will not select as reviewers individuals employed by or engaged to represent a Company.
6fe37c80-9b19-43d3-b12e-0c98fc1d63d9.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

e41c88ee-41fb-4240-959a-21ec98c66ad7.text: F. AASLD will require that Guideline recommendations, Guidance statements or Quality Measures be subject to multiple levels of review, including rigorous peer-review by a range of experts. AASLD will not select as reviewers individuals employed by or engaged to represent a Company. G. AASLD will publish Guideline/Guidance or Quality Measures development writing panel/group members’ disclosure information adjacent to each Guideline/Guidance or Quality Measures and will identify voting members.
e41c88ee-41fb-4240-959a-21ec98c66ad7.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
e41c88ee-41fb-4240-959a-21ec98c66ad7.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

7bfd4f4c-42a7-4f58-a295-afa11c83e7b4.text: AASLD will not select as reviewers individuals employed by or engaged to represent a Company. G. AASLD will publish Guideline/Guidance or Quality Measures development writing panel/group members’ disclosure information adjacent to each Guideline/Guidance or Quality Measures and will identify voting members. H. AASLD will require all Guideline/Guidance or Quality Measures contributors, including expert advisors or reviewers who are not officially part of a Guideline/Guidance or Quality Measures development writing panel/group, to disclose financial or other substantive relationships that may constitute conflicts of interest.

H. AASLD will require all Guideline/Guidance or Quality Measures contributors, including expert advisors or reviewers who are not officially part of a Guideline/Guidance or Quality Measures development writing panel/group, to disclose financial or other substantive relationships that may constitute conflicts of interest. 4.

Adherence to the Code Adherence to this Code will be promulgated by AASLD. All Members of the AASLD will be encouraged to adopt the principles of this Code and their application.

dccf2796-f9d7-40bd-9ce9-b6393a319bb8.text: Adherence to the Code Adherence to this Code will be promulgated by AASLD. All Members of the AASLD will be encouraged to adopt the principles of this Code and their application. For AASLD officials, Committee Chairs and members, willful non-reporting or withholding of COI information with the intention to conceal ongoing or planned non-permitted activities and/or failure to respond to AASLD requests to complete or clarify COI reporting and/or to divest from specific non-permitted activities in a timely manner may result in disciplinary action by the Governing Board.
dccf2796-f9d7-40bd-9ce9-b6393a319bb8.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
dccf2796-f9d7-40bd-9ce9-b6393a319bb8.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

78845b10-0048-43b2-a41c-df7d71bf13c3.text: All Members of the AASLD will be encouraged to adopt the principles of this Code and their application. For AASLD officials, Committee Chairs and members, willful non-reporting or withholding of COI information with the intention to conceal ongoing or planned non-permitted activities and/or failure to respond to AASLD requests to complete or clarify COI reporting and/or to divest from specific non-permitted activities in a timely manner may result in disciplinary action by the Governing Board. These actions may include reprimand, removal from current Committee position(s), exclusion from future service on Committees, and/or suspension of membership in the AASLD.
78845b10-0048-43b2-a41c-df7d71bf13c3.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
78845b10-0048-43b2-a41c-df7d71bf13c3.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

902c6264-18fe-42f2-bb96-07083b431fcd.text: For AASLD officials, Committee Chairs and members, willful non-reporting or withholding of COI information with the intention to conceal ongoing or planned non-permitted activities and/or failure to respond to AASLD requests to complete or clarify COI reporting and/or to divest from specific non-permitted activities in a timely manner may result in disciplinary action by the Governing Board. These actions may include reprimand, removal from current Committee position(s), exclusion from future service on Committees, and/or suspension of membership in the AASLD. Appendices APPENDIX A Conflict of Interest: Special Considerations for Practice Guidelines Committee and Practice Metrics Committee Chairs, Committee Members and Writing Group Members Disclosures Disclosures must be made at least annually and when material changes occur.

Proportionality requires a policy to address the most important conflicts (i.e.
d945f383-2c1c-4a0a-b07e-5a68b56d2328.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
d945f383-2c1c-4a0a-b07e-5a68b56d2328.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

453f5367-4fa3-4931-b86f-665d1fd1d1e3.text: ](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/sites/default/files/2022-05/Appendix%20B%20Table%202%20from%20Revised%20Code%20for%20COI%20-%20June%202021%20Update.pdf) APPENDIX C Policy and Criteria for Assessment of Conflicts of Interest by the Ethics Committee The Ethics Committee will employ a policy of evaluating conflicts of interest that embodies the criteria of proportionality, transparency, accountability and fairness. Proportionality requires a policy to address the most important conflicts (i.e. direct financial relationships or financial relationships in-kind); the disclosure forms will reflect this policy.
453f5367-4fa3-4931-b86f-665d1fd1d1e3.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
453f5367-4fa3-4931-b86f-665d1fd1d1e3.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

30692fd5-f11d-499f-8dd9-f9595a6d61c1.text: Proportionality requires a policy to address the most important conflicts (i.e. direct financial relationships or financial relationships in-kind); the disclosure forms will reflect this policy. Transparency dictates that the policy be available and understood by the affected parties; this code and the attached appendices (outlining the criteria that will be employed for the assessment of conflicts) will be posted for all members to review and disclosure forms will be accompanied by clear instructions to allow members to understand the specific information required to fulfill full and accurate disclosure.
30692fd5-f11d-499f-8dd9-f9595a6d61c1.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
30692fd5-f11d-499f-8dd9-f9595a6d61c1.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

9c4a092e-0320-44fb-a844-dba34333b0ce.text: direct financial relationships or financial relationships in-kind); the disclosure forms will reflect this policy. Transparency dictates that the policy be available and understood by the affected parties; this code and the attached appendices (outlining the criteria that will be employed for the assessment of conflicts) will be posted for all members to review and disclosure forms will be accompanied by clear instructions to allow members to understand the specific information required to fulfill full and accurate disclosure. Accountability requires divulging the details of who will be responsible for assessment and enforcement; this is reflected in the code stating that the Ethics Committee will be responsible for the assessment of conflicts and that enforcement of the management of conflicts will be via relevant sections of the Association’s By-laws and the joint actions of the Ethics Committee and the Governing Board.
9c4a092e-0320-44fb-a844-dba34333b0ce.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
9c4a092e-0320-44fb-a844-dba34333b0ce.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

79373abd-4fff-4dae-899d-d7727c2e22a6.text: Transparency dictates that the policy be available and understood by the affected parties; this code and the attached appendices (outlining the criteria that will be employed for the assessment of conflicts) will be posted for all members to review and disclosure forms will be accompanied by clear instructions to allow members to understand the specific information required to fulfill full and accurate disclosure. Accountability requires divulging the details of who will be responsible for assessment and enforcement; this is reflected in the code stating that the Ethics Committee will be responsible for the assessment of conflicts and that enforcement of the management of conflicts will be via relevant sections of the Association’s By-laws and the joint actions of the Ethics Committee and the Governing Board. Fairness requires that a policy for the evaluation of conflicts apply to all relevant parties with equal measure; this criterion is met by the statement in this code indicating that the criteria employed to assess conflicts of interest will be independent of the individual or groups to whom they are applied but rather will vary by the set of circumstances around which a conflict arises.
79373abd-4fff-4dae-899d-d7727c2e22a6.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
79373abd-4fff-4dae-899d-d7727c2e22a6.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

a7bdd0fa-1680-4180-907d-0b7633814267.text: Accountability requires divulging the details of who will be responsible for assessment and enforcement; this is reflected in the code stating that the Ethics Committee will be responsible for the assessment of conflicts and that enforcement of the management of conflicts will be via relevant sections of the Association’s By-laws and the joint actions of the Ethics Committee and the Governing Board. Fairness requires that a policy for the evaluation of conflicts apply to all relevant parties with equal measure; this criterion is met by the statement in this code indicating that the criteria employed to assess conflicts of interest will be independent of the individual or groups to whom they are applied but rather will vary by the set of circumstances around which a conflict arises. Criteria for Assessment of Conflicts of Interest Conflicts of interest are rarely, if ever, “absent” or “clearly present”, but rather vary in their severity and relevance to a particular circumstance.
a7bdd0fa-1680-4180-907d-0b7633814267.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
a7bdd0fa-1680-4180-907d-0b7633814267.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

12f85be0-6b37-4c05-9c26-3ee7ae5be7fe.text: Fairness requires that a policy for the evaluation of conflicts apply to all relevant parties with equal measure; this criterion is met by the statement in this code indicating that the criteria employed to assess conflicts of interest will be independent of the individual or groups to whom they are applied but rather will vary by the set of circumstances around which a conflict arises. Criteria for Assessment of Conflicts of Interest Conflicts of interest are rarely, if ever, “absent” or “clearly present”, but rather vary in their severity and relevance to a particular circumstance. Accordingly, the Ethics Committee will assess potential conflicts from the perspectives of the chance that a conflict will impair impartial decision-making and the degree of harm that would arise from such impairment.
12f85be0-6b37-4c05-9c26-3ee7ae5be7fe.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
12f85be0-6b37-4c05-9c26-3ee7ae5be7fe.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

e90a09c9-93df-44f0-962e-7788c540b626.text: Criteria for Assessment of Conflicts of Interest Conflicts of interest are rarely, if ever, “absent” or “clearly present”, but rather vary in their severity and relevance to a particular circumstance. Accordingly, the Ethics Committee will assess potential conflicts from the perspectives of the chance that a conflict will impair impartial decision-making and the degree of harm that would arise from such impairment. Based on this Code’s definition of a conflict of interest as any circumstances that create a potential risk that professional judgments or actions regarding a primary interest will be unduly influenced by a secondary interest, the Ethics Committee will use the following criteria when assessing a conflict: What is the financial value of the secondary interest involved?
e90a09c9-93df-44f0-962e-7788c540b626.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
e90a09c9-93df-44f0-962e-7788c540b626.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

58bf55ba-3687-4b71-b5be-8c52453bf9ca.text: Accordingly, the Ethics Committee will assess potential conflicts from the perspectives of the chance that a conflict will impair impartial decision-making and the degree of harm that would arise from such impairment. Based on this Code’s definition of a conflict of interest as any circumstances that create a potential risk that professional judgments or actions regarding a primary interest will be unduly influenced by a secondary interest, the Ethics Committee will use the following criteria when assessing a conflict: What is the financial value of the secondary interest involved? What is the scope of the relationship(s) of the individual being assessed, with the party or parties associated with the secondary interest?
58bf55ba-3687-4b71-b5be-8c52453bf9ca.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
58bf55ba-3687-4b71-b5be-8c52453bf9ca.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

84cafde8-01de-4368-8ec7-16064af66e32.text: Based on this Code’s definition of a conflict of interest as any circumstances that create a potential risk that professional judgments or actions regarding a primary interest will be unduly influenced by a secondary interest, the Ethics Committee will use the following criteria when assessing a conflict: What is the financial value of the secondary interest involved? What is the scope of the relationship(s) of the individual being assessed, with the party or parties associated with the secondary interest? Is the circumstance one involving sole discretion of the particular individual being assessed?
84cafde8-01de-4368-8ec7-16064af66e32.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
84cafde8-01de-4368-8ec7-16064af66e32.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

1e39656c-a86c-49a4-b372-6f1bf204f3b0.text: What is the scope of the relationship(s) of the individual being assessed, with the party or parties associated with the secondary interest? Is the circumstance one involving sole discretion of the particular individual being assessed? What is the value (either direct financial or “in-kind”) to the AASLD of the interest that could be affected by a conflict? What is the scope of the consequence to the AASLD resulting from public disclosure of the conflict?
1e39656c-a86c-49a4-b372-6f1bf204f3b0.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
1e39656c-a86c-49a4-b372-6f1bf204f3b0.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

c3db2133-6661-4be0-aac8-f158aaefeba5.text: What is the value (either direct financial or “in-kind”) to the AASLD of the interest that could be affected by a conflict? What is the scope of the consequence to the AASLD resulting from public disclosure of the conflict? Questions 1, 2 and 3 evaluate the potential that a conflict could impair impartial decision-making, while questions 4 and 5 assess the degree of harm that could result from an impairment.
c3db2133-6661-4be0-aac8-f158aaefeba5.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
c3db2133-6661-4be0-aac8-f158aaefeba5.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

771c8dba-4160-4a39-922c-f4a9e1396feb.text: What is the scope of the consequence to the AASLD resulting from public disclosure of the conflict? Questions 1, 2 and 3 evaluate the potential that a conflict could impair impartial decision-making, while questions 4 and 5 assess the degree of harm that could result from an impairment. After review and discussion of an individual’s potential conflict(s) of interest and its (their) severity, the Ethics Committee will, by voting according to this Code, determine that a conflict exists and that the severity of the conflict rises to the point that management of the conflict requires more than simple disclosure.
771c8dba-4160-4a39-922c-f4a9e1396feb.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
771c8dba-4160-4a39-922c-f4a9e1396feb.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

49a36ac0-733d-4f0e-a25f-79bcd4239ebb.text: Questions 1, 2 and 3 evaluate the potential that a conflict could impair impartial decision-making, while questions 4 and 5 assess the degree of harm that could result from an impairment. After review and discussion of an individual’s potential conflict(s) of interest and its (their) severity, the Ethics Committee will, by voting according to this Code, determine that a conflict exists and that the severity of the conflict rises to the point that management of the conflict requires more than simple disclosure. Based on the criteria outlined above, the Ethics Committee, by a two-thirds majority vote, will request recusal, withdrawal of consideration for a nominated position, AASLD-independent review, divestiture or any other appropriate management of the conflict.
49a36ac0-733d-4f0e-a25f-79bcd4239ebb.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
49a36ac0-733d-4f0e-a25f-79bcd4239ebb.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

49c73471-5c3a-4077-bfb6-8abf7aad2f62.text: After review and discussion of an individual’s potential conflict(s) of interest and its (their) severity, the Ethics Committee will, by voting according to this Code, determine that a conflict exists and that the severity of the conflict rises to the point that management of the conflict requires more than simple disclosure. Based on the criteria outlined above, the Ethics Committee, by a two-thirds majority vote, will request recusal, withdrawal of consideration for a nominated position, AASLD-independent review, divestiture or any other appropriate management of the conflict. The recommendation of the Ethics Committee will be forwarded to the Governing Board for its approval and enforcement.
49c73471-5c3a-4077-bfb6-8abf7aad2f62.fileName: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD
49c73471-5c3a-4077-bfb6-8abf7aad2f62.topic: Code for the Assessment and Management of Conflict of Interest | AASLD

1470a4ca-b5c5-4908-9eec-38bc1d85288f.text: Based on the criteria outlined above, the Ethics Committee, by a two-thirds majority vote, will request recusal, withdrawal of consideration for a nominated position, AASLD-independent review, divestiture or any other appropriate management of the conflict. The recommendation of the Ethics Committee will be forwarded to the Governing Board for its approval and enforcement. If the matter involves an individual who is a Member of the Governing Board, that Member will recuse themselves from any deliberations involving that matter.

[Home](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/) Governing Board Governing Board Purpose The Governing Board will manage the affairs of the association, and review, establish, and modify professional scientific business administrative policies within the limits of the Bylaws to further the purposes of AASLD.

6486bfed-e6eb-469c-a0fd-22b7ce71622b.text: The Governing Board shall approve an annual budget, establish financial goals for the Association, oversee the financial operations of the Association, and have discretion in the control, management of investments and disbursement of funds. The Board shall establish and review long-term objectives of AASLD and establish the priority of all programs and activities.

51670bf8-48d7-42e1-b5da-9eb4e19e9bec.text: Composition The Board consists of a President, President-Elect, Secretary, Treasurer, and seven Councilors (three elected as regular Councilors, three elected as Councilors-at-Large and the Past-President). Responsibilities Establish and disband appointed groups including committees (not established by the Bylaws) and task forces. Designate representatives to other organizations. Receive reports from all standing committees and approve candidates for membership.

e5c004cc-b874-49aa-a283-4feb39fe2d9c.text: Designate representatives to other organizations. Receive reports from all standing committees and approve candidates for membership. Oversee and manage the publication of the Associations' journals: HEPATOLOGY, Liver Transplantation, Clinical Liver Disease, and Hepatology Communications, as well as all other AASLD publications. Administer selection of all official awards, prizes and grants. Accept grants, contributions, gifts, bequests or devices to further the purposes of AASLD.

e97a0863-a8ee-4638-8809-03af9d105199.text: Administer selection of all official awards, prizes and grants. Accept grants, contributions, gifts, bequests or devices to further the purposes of AASLD. Approve or disapprove all recommendations of standing committees. Approve all nominations to standing committees. Investigate all charges of unprofessional or unbecoming conduct made against any member or to bring such charges on its own initiative. Executive Committee The Executive Committee exercises the power of the Governing Board between meetings.

01456625-0ace-44af-ab4a-bc96692a1ede.text: Investigate all charges of unprofessional or unbecoming conduct made against any member or to bring such charges on its own initiative. Executive Committee The Executive Committee exercises the power of the Governing Board between meetings. It includes the President, President-Elect, Secretary, Treasurer, and Senior Councilor (i.e., the Councilor serving the third year of his/her term) and the immediate Past-President.

0fa67aee-87f2-4398-977d-dd6754155782.text: AASLD is not only keeping up, we are leading the way. As the needs of our members – and the larger hepatology community – evolve, so do our strategies to meet those needs. AASLD's Governing Board is committed to advancing bold initiatives to future-proof our society. With continuous progress and measurement to ensure we are meeting our goals, AASLD is forging a new path to support the hepatology community in advancing the science of liver disease.

8a2db82b-8eb1-4737-879c-38b805a9a88f.text: AASLD's Governing Board is committed to advancing bold initiatives to future-proof our society. With continuous progress and measurement to ensure we are meeting our goals, AASLD is forging a new path to support the hepatology community in advancing the science of liver disease. Mission Statement To advance and disseminate the science and practice of hepatology, and to promote liver health and quality patient care. Vision To prevent and cure liver disease.

a5b75d58-b60a-4d24-9e19-2ce1aa505d29.text: Mission Statement To advance and disseminate the science and practice of hepatology, and to promote liver health and quality patient care. Vision To prevent and cure liver disease. Values Impactful | Inclusive | Innovative | Responsive Create Innovative Organizational Culture Enhance AASLD’s membership benefits portfolio focusing on member value & experience Accelerate AASLD’s digital transformation and innovative delivery of research and education to global hepatology community Seek, evaluate and execute opportunities in data-driven and financially prudent fashion [Practice Inclusiveness and Achieve Equity](https://www.aasld.org/diversity-equity-and-inclusion) Ensure patient perspective is reflected in society’s work Drive engagement in AASLD’s signature programs from diverse group of global participants Deliver the most inclusive member experiences with focus on underrepresented, early career and advance practice populations Advance Scientific Leadership in Hepatology Champion research funding through AASLD Foundation support and advocate for funding investment Present best science in hepatology in journals and meetings Lead global effort to accelerate evidence-based care for patients with MASH/MASLD worldwide [﻿Here](https://www.aasld.org/sites/default/files/2025-02/image.jpeg) is a printable version of our strategic plan.

c134a556-ff9c-460c-8010-77a3a742d124.fileName: Elevating Liver Health Advocacy: AASLD's Impact on Capitol Hill | AASLD
c134a556-ff9c-460c-8010-77a3a742d124.topic: Elevating Liver Health Advocacy: AASLD's Impact on Capitol Hill | AASLD

71d0e291-0ca2-4ff8-b049-765950de5b8a.text: Our recent Capitol Hill Day on March 20, 2024, was a pivotal moment where we amplified our voice and advocated for critical initiatives to transform the landscape of liver disease prevention and treatment in the United States. Key Initiatives Supported National Plan to Eliminate Hepatitis C (HCV): AASLD spearheaded efforts to garner support for authorizing legislation aimed at implementing the national plan to eliminate hepatitis C (HCV) within five years.
71d0e291-0ca2-4ff8-b049-765950de5b8a.fileName: Elevating Liver Health Advocacy: AASLD's Impact on Capitol Hill | AASLD
71d0e291-0ca2-4ff8-b049-765950de5b8a.topic: Elevating Liver Health Advocacy: AASLD's Impact on Capitol Hill | AASLD

54b4bed7-97a7-4fa7-8010-0098ed18f347.text: This ambitious plan is crucial in combating the prevalence of HCV and reducing its burden on public health. Updated Nomenclature for Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Steatotic Liver Disease (MASLD): Recognizing the importance of accurate terminology in medical discourse, AASLD advocated for the adoption of [updated nomenclature for Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Steatotic Liver Disease (MASLD)](https://www.aasld.org/new-masld-nomenclature) .
54b4bed7-97a7-4fa7-8010-0098ed18f347.fileName: Elevating Liver Health Advocacy: AASLD's Impact on Capitol Hill | AASLD
54b4bed7-97a7-4fa7-8010-0098ed18f347.topic: Elevating Liver Health Advocacy: AASLD's Impact on Capitol Hill | AASLD

96b8e34a-bab3-46c3-b1d5-51145a5b1f3f.text: This initiative aims to streamline communication and enhance understanding in the medical community. Funding for National Institutes of Health (NIH): AASLD rallied behind the allocation of at least $51.303 billion for the National Institutes of Health (NIH) in the Labor-HHS bill for FY 2025. This funding is vital for advancing liver-related research and innovation, paving the way for groundbreaking discoveries and treatments.
96b8e34a-bab3-46c3-b1d5-51145a5b1f3f.fileName: Elevating Liver Health Advocacy: AASLD's Impact on Capitol Hill | AASLD
96b8e34a-bab3-46c3-b1d5-51145a5b1f3f.topic: Elevating Liver Health Advocacy: AASLD's Impact on Capitol Hill | AASLD

6de1105c-0c31-46cc-bc83-05092bc39bbb.text: Funding for National Institutes of Health (NIH): AASLD rallied behind the allocation of at least $51.303 billion for the National Institutes of Health (NIH) in the Labor-HHS bill for FY 2025. This funding is vital for advancing liver-related research and innovation, paving the way for groundbreaking discoveries and treatments. Support for CDC Division of Viral Hepatitis (DVH): In our advocacy efforts, AASLD advocated for $150 million in funding for the CDC Division of Viral Hepatitis (DVH) in the Labor-HHS bill for FY 2025.
6de1105c-0c31-46cc-bc83-05092bc39bbb.fileName: Elevating Liver Health Advocacy: AASLD's Impact on Capitol Hill | AASLD
6de1105c-0c31-46cc-bc83-05092bc39bbb.topic: Elevating Liver Health Advocacy: AASLD's Impact on Capitol Hill | AASLD

28d26f33-389d-46d9-87df-137b804933dc.text: This funding is vital for advancing liver-related research and innovation, paving the way for groundbreaking discoveries and treatments. Support for CDC Division of Viral Hepatitis (DVH): In our advocacy efforts, AASLD advocated for $150 million in funding for the CDC Division of Viral Hepatitis (DVH) in the Labor-HHS bill for FY 2025. This funding is essential for bolstering public health initiatives aimed at preventing and controlling viral hepatitis outbreaks.

bf797204-e020-4269-8370-8e3a2ffd4dac.text: Support for CDC Division of Viral Hepatitis (DVH): In our advocacy efforts, AASLD advocated for $150 million in funding for the CDC Division of Viral Hepatitis (DVH) in the Labor-HHS bill for FY 2025. This funding is essential for bolstering public health initiatives aimed at preventing and controlling viral hepatitis outbreaks. Congressional Hepatitis Caucus: AASLD urged Representatives to join the Congressional Hepatitis Caucus, a bipartisan platform dedicated to raising awareness, promoting education, and driving legislative action to address the challenges posed by hepatitis.

By The Numbers Capitol Hill Day was a rousing success. Here's a breakdown of the day: 55 meetings executed 31 AASLD members participated, including: 4 Board Members 12 SIG Leaders 12 patient advocates 5 ELS Scholars 6 AASLD staff Capitol Hill Day Flyers Educating policymakers on the impact of budget appropriations and votes as it relates to liver disease is really important.

Imaging Biopsy Yes No Check Hepatitis C Ab with Reflex to PCR Hepatitis BsAg, sAb, cAb Ceruloplasmin lmmunoglobulins ANA, ASMA, AMA Iron Studies Celiac Antibodies Alpha-1-AT levels OR Rule out other underlying etiologies of liver diseases The patient has steatotic liver disease. Does the adult patient meet any one of the cardiometabolic criteria?

71081019-31ef-4b0e-86f2-c240239121f1.text: Imaging Biopsy Yes No Check Hepatitis C Ab with Reflex to PCR Hepatitis BsAg, sAb, cAb Ceruloplasmin lmmunoglobulins ANA, ASMA, AMA Iron Studies Celiac Antibodies Alpha-1-AT levels OR Rule out other underlying etiologies of liver diseases The patient has steatotic liver disease. Does the adult patient meet any one of the cardiometabolic criteria? BMI ≥ 25 kg/m 2 [23 Asia] OR WC> 94 cm (M) 80 cm (F) OR ethnicity adjusted Fasting serum glucose ≥ 5.6 mmol/L [100 mg/dL] OR 2-hour post-load glucose levels ≥ 7.8 mmol/L [≥140 mg/dL] OR HbA1c ≥ 5.7% [39 mmol/L] OR type 2 diabetes OR treatment for type 2 diabetes Blood pressure ≥ 130/85 mmHg OR specific antihypertensive drug treatment Plasma triglycerides ≥ 1.70 mmol/L [150 mg/dL] OR lipid lowering treatment Plasma HDL-cholesterol ≤ 1.0 mmol/L [40 mg/dL] (M) and ≤ 1.3 mmol/L [50 mg/dL] (F) OR lipid lowering treatment Yes No Does the patient have features suggestive of cirrhosis?

88b2b514-261f-4e6c-9b89-a95ff076336d.text: [Consulte Mais Informação](https://aasldv2022dev.aasld.org/nova-nomenclatura-esteatose-hepatica) Special thanks to our friends and ALEH and CASL for their help with the MASLD translations! About Steatotic Liver Disease (SLD) AASLD and its members are proud to have been one of the leading multinational liver societies that developed and finalized the new nomenclature for liver disease, which was [announced in June 2023. ](https://journals.lww.com/hep/10.1097/HEP.0000000000000520) What to know about the new nomenclature: Steatotic liver disease (SLD) was chosen as an overarching term to encompass the various aetiologies of steatosis.

c1640b8e-7920-4558-b54a-64828c748220.text: About Steatotic Liver Disease (SLD) AASLD and its members are proud to have been one of the leading multinational liver societies that developed and finalized the new nomenclature for liver disease, which was [announced in June 2023. ](https://journals.lww.com/hep/10.1097/HEP.0000000000000520) What to know about the new nomenclature: Steatotic liver disease (SLD) was chosen as an overarching term to encompass the various aetiologies of steatosis. The term steatohepatitis was felt to be an important pathophysiological concept that should be retained.

d8e8dd22-72a2-4ee0-8669-d6a2f5a5962a.text: ](https://journals.lww.com/hep/10.1097/HEP.0000000000000520) What to know about the new nomenclature: Steatotic liver disease (SLD) was chosen as an overarching term to encompass the various aetiologies of steatosis. The term steatohepatitis was felt to be an important pathophysiological concept that should be retained. Nonalcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD) will now be metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease (MASLD).

511c3b0c-56af-4480-90c6-5290fdf486a1.text: The term steatohepatitis was felt to be an important pathophysiological concept that should be retained. Nonalcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD) will now be metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease (MASLD). MASLD encompasses patients who have hepatic steatosis and have at least one of five cardiometabolic risk factors.

35cff5f7-3931-4694-801e-f2bbf5eef3ba.text: Nonalcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD) will now be metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease (MASLD). MASLD encompasses patients who have hepatic steatosis and have at least one of five cardiometabolic risk factors. A new category, outside pure MASLD, termed MetALD (pronunciation: Met A-L-D) was selected to describe those with MASLD who consume greater amounts of alcohol per week (140 g/week and 210 g/week for females and males respectively).

47c39164-03cf-4ac2-a0ef-65d7e8206f64.text: MASLD encompasses patients who have hepatic steatosis and have at least one of five cardiometabolic risk factors. A new category, outside pure MASLD, termed MetALD (pronunciation: Met A-L-D) was selected to describe those with MASLD who consume greater amounts of alcohol per week (140 g/week and 210 g/week for females and males respectively). Metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis (MASH) is the replacement term for NASH. Those with no metabolic parameters and no known cause have cryptogenic SLD.

e52dc99e-d0fb-42a0-ad1a-6549fb6f7f84.text: Metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis (MASH) is the replacement term for NASH. Those with no metabolic parameters and no known cause have cryptogenic SLD. Steatotic Liver Disease Classifications Norah Terrault, MD, MPH, FAASLD, Discusses the New Nomenclature Remote video URL Introducing MASLD and MetALD MASLD, formerly known as NAFLD, is the [most common chronic liver disease](https://www.niddk.nih.gov/health-information/liver-disease/nafld-nash/definition-facts) around the world, affecting more than 30% of global population.

eaa05683-03ad-4848-aee7-949879c093dc.text: Those with no metabolic parameters and no known cause have cryptogenic SLD. Steatotic Liver Disease Classifications Norah Terrault, MD, MPH, FAASLD, Discusses the New Nomenclature Remote video URL Introducing MASLD and MetALD MASLD, formerly known as NAFLD, is the [most common chronic liver disease](https://www.niddk.nih.gov/health-information/liver-disease/nafld-nash/definition-facts) around the world, affecting more than 30% of global population. This was why it was vital that the global liver community coalesce around an affirmative, non-stigmatizing name and diagnosis.

6f139360-f5bb-4e7d-a730-4e7320cd82e4.text: Steatotic Liver Disease Classifications Norah Terrault, MD, MPH, FAASLD, Discusses the New Nomenclature Remote video URL Introducing MASLD and MetALD MASLD, formerly known as NAFLD, is the [most common chronic liver disease](https://www.niddk.nih.gov/health-information/liver-disease/nafld-nash/definition-facts) around the world, affecting more than 30% of global population. This was why it was vital that the global liver community coalesce around an affirmative, non-stigmatizing name and diagnosis. Ultimately, the global members of the Nomenclature Development Initiative were focused on ensuring the global community had better nomenclature that could be used around the world so that the research and funding could be better directed to save more people’s lives.

52a135dd-6111-41fc-99a4-d7c7b5976dd3.text: This was why it was vital that the global liver community coalesce around an affirmative, non-stigmatizing name and diagnosis. Ultimately, the global members of the Nomenclature Development Initiative were focused on ensuring the global community had better nomenclature that could be used around the world so that the research and funding could be better directed to save more people’s lives. After six stages, including four online surveys and two in-person meetings, the average response rate was more than 75% for the four rounds of data collection with 89% final response rate and 85% of whom ultimately approved the recommendation.

d39d3d87-b4e0-413a-98b6-8e4d879465a1.text: Ultimately, the global members of the Nomenclature Development Initiative were focused on ensuring the global community had better nomenclature that could be used around the world so that the research and funding could be better directed to save more people’s lives. After six stages, including four online surveys and two in-person meetings, the average response rate was more than 75% for the four rounds of data collection with 89% final response rate and 85% of whom ultimately approved the recommendation. We are also proud of the numerous societies and organizations that endorsed the recommendations .

79c16f9d-7be3-467b-972f-d59a343c2c34.text: After six stages, including four online surveys and two in-person meetings, the average response rate was more than 75% for the four rounds of data collection with 89% final response rate and 85% of whom ultimately approved the recommendation. We are also proud of the numerous societies and organizations that endorsed the recommendations . As the transparent and collaborative Delphi process identified and agreed upon , we will no longer use the previously exclusionary, negative and confounder terms that used potentially stigmatizing language of nonalcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD) and nonalcoholic steatohepatitis (NASH) .

c84e385d-ae2c-46f7-88b9-72d52c543f47.text: We are also proud of the numerous societies and organizations that endorsed the recommendations . As the transparent and collaborative Delphi process identified and agreed upon , we will no longer use the previously exclusionary, negative and confounder terms that used potentially stigmatizing language of nonalcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD) and nonalcoholic steatohepatitis (NASH) . The Delphi panel understands that not everyone agrees that this is an issue.

d40f43b5-0dc2-421d-b903-d0a184ef77d9.text: As the transparent and collaborative Delphi process identified and agreed upon , we will no longer use the previously exclusionary, negative and confounder terms that used potentially stigmatizing language of nonalcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD) and nonalcoholic steatohepatitis (NASH) . The Delphi panel understands that not everyone agrees that this is an issue. The tolerable threshold for the amount of people that feel stigmatized is not for anyone to determine. Simply, if it can be avoided, it should be.

a9d77ef8-5f27-48e4-8397-df14e71e6784.text: The tolerable threshold for the amount of people that feel stigmatized is not for anyone to determine. Simply, if it can be avoided, it should be. With the new nomenclature, we now have an affirmative name and diagnosis without using stigmatizing language. But to be clear: SLD does not alter natural history, clinical trials or biomarkers nor will it impede development. The staging and severity that we use today will stay the same too.

7ef8189b-6c06-482a-8ad3-17d0c3857605.text: But to be clear: SLD does not alter natural history, clinical trials or biomarkers nor will it impede development. The staging and severity that we use today will stay the same too. The Delphi panel has defined and outlined a group that has not been studied (MetALD), who will benefit from being included in clinical trials and integrated into care pathways.

a940890d-bc15-4a96-bf7a-8f00731409ed.text: The staging and severity that we use today will stay the same too. The Delphi panel has defined and outlined a group that has not been studied (MetALD), who will benefit from being included in clinical trials and integrated into care pathways. There is more work to be done to increase disease awareness, reduce stigma and accelerate drug and biomarker development to benefit patients with MASLD and MetALD .

2899c8cb-a7f7-4e5a-8af2-88df6f76c0aa.text: The Delphi panel has defined and outlined a group that has not been studied (MetALD), who will benefit from being included in clinical trials and integrated into care pathways. There is more work to be done to increase disease awareness, reduce stigma and accelerate drug and biomarker development to benefit patients with MASLD and MetALD .
